title: #partnerships: 4161234567
preview:
Renee Gomez: starting a thread to align on marketplace certification, SKU mapping, and go-to-market for the Snowflake connector + Databricks delta path + Milvus vector backend. want a crisp checklist and owners.
Ethan Park: can take certification owner. main asks: security questionnaire, perf benchmarks, and marketplace listing metadata.
Noah Singh: i'll handle SKU mapping and pricing tiers. need input on token quotas vs. document ingest pricing.
Lina Chen: eng will own perf runs and a reproducible benchmark repo. what's the target lab environment?
Talia (Snowflake PM): happy to coordinate Snowflake listing requirements. we need conformance tests for COPY INTO, delta-stream compatibility, and an auth flow that supports OAuth + keypair.
Renee Gomez: timeline proposal: test plan + artifacts by 06/02, perf runs 06/08-06/10, legal/compliance signoff 06/12, marketplace listing draft 06/15. feedback?
Ethan Park: makes sense. add a smoke test for role-based access and row-level security (RLS) compliance.
Noah Singh: SKU mapping idea: 1) dev tier (limited throughput), 2) pilot tier (reserved capacity), 3) enterprise (dedicated + SLA). map to marketplace SKUs and Redwood Dedicated plans.
Lina Chen: benchmark env: 4x a100 equivalent for vector path, Snowflake trial account with sample DB of 200GB, Databricks cluster with autoscaling workers. will prepare infra as terraform module.
Talia (Snowflake PM): Snowflake needs a manifest of connector endpoints and an architecture diagram for listing. please include data residency notes.
marketplace-bot: checklist created: "marketplace-cert-snow-dbx-milvus"  owner: Ethan Park  due: 2026-06-02
Ethan Park: legal asks: PII handling, KMS integration, DPA draft. Marketing will need a 1-pager and screenshots. Can marketing provide a template?
Renee Gomez: marketing template link: https://redwood.internal/mkt/templates/connector-onepager.md
Noah Singh: pricing detail: propose per-document ingest + per-embedding unit. need to estimate token-equivalent for vector indexing to surface cost to customers.
Lina Chen: perf targets suggested: vector insert >= 3000 docs/min, query p95 < 150ms at 50qps, Snowflake ingestion latency < 8min for 2GB batch. are those acceptable?
Talia (Snowflake PM): Snowflake OK with those as pilot targets. we will require a failover test where writes switch to a secondary account.
Ethan Park: security gating: run an internal security review, a third-party pentest scheduled, and a conformance scan for dependencies. add these to the checklist.
Renee Gomez: who's owning the pentest coordination?
Noah Singh: i can coordinate pentest vendor engagement. need a scope doc: API surfaces, gateway, and connector installers.
Lina Chen: for installers, note we support both sidecar and standalone binary modes. installer checklist: `sha256` verification, signed packages, and upgrade rollback plan.
marketplace-bot: calendar event: "connector perf run: milvus path" 2026-06-08T09:00Z  attendees: Lina, Ethan, Noah
Ethan Park: marketplace metadata: we need tags (connectors, data-platforms, vectors), pricing tiers, support SLAs, and trial credits. Noah please draft SKU names.
Noah Singh: draft SKUs: RD-Connector-Dev, RD-Connector-Pilot, RD-Connector-Enterprise. will add price bands and sample billing examples.
Talia (Snowflake PM): compliance note  if pilot customers use cross-region replication, we need to document egress and residency controls. include a short howto.
Renee Gomez: marketing & legal: ensure the 1-pager has a data-flow diagram with PII handling boxes labeled.
Lina Chen: engineering runbook items: benchmark script (`bench/run_milvus.sh`), perf result uploader (`ci/upload_perf.sh`), and an automated conformance test harness. linking repo soon.
Ethan Park: acceptance criteria for listing: 1) pass perf targets, 2) security review closed, 3) legal signoff on DPA, 4) marketplace metadata complete, 5) customer trial ready.
Noah Singh: ops note: we should expose telemetry metrics for marketplace customers only (tenant-scoped) and include per-SKU limits enforced in the gateway.
Talia (Snowflake PM): Snowflake marketplace policy also requires a partner support contact and an official naming convention. will send guidelines.
Renee Gomez: can we add a quick beta cohort list? suggest 5 pilot customers from banking and e-com verticals. Noah pls draft invites.
Noah Singh: will draft pilot invite email and onboarding steps.
Lina Chen: will publish initial perf results to the runbook after internal runs, tagging anomalies.
Ethan Park: legal/compliance: assign Sarah and Vikram for review. need timelines from them to lock the 06/12 signoff.
marketplace-bot: reminder: checklist review due 2026-06-02T23:59Z
Renee Gomez: OK next steps: Ethan finalize checklist, Noah draft SKUs + pilot invites, Lina provision benchmark infra, Talia send Snowflake listing guidelines. reconvene weekly on Fridays.
Ethan Park: noted. i'll push the checklist by EOD and ping legal.
Noah Singh: thanks all. i'll share pricing examples and a sample invoice line item.
Lina Chen: thanks team, starting infra provisioning now. will post the terraform module link when ready.
Talia (Snowflake PM): ping me when the architecture diagram is ready, i'll fast-track the listing review.
Renee Gomez: locking thread for today  DM me for urgent items. :raised_hands:

title: #partnerships: 3950000001
preview:
Elena Park: quick sync — we need a concrete onboarding plan for KMS/HSM and secrets manager partners who want a marketplace listing + SI integration. two main asks: a technical validation lab (remote) and a simple billing/attribution pattern for deals. can we agree on scope today?
Mateo Alvarez: for the lab, require two HSM access patterns: PKCS#11 for on-prem HSMs and a BYOK HTTP/KMS flow for cloud KMS. must validate key-wrap and unwrap, plus rotation. suggest we publish a minimal test harness (docker-compose) partners can run against our sandbox.
Claire Dubois: security checklist high-level: FIPS mode proof (if available), attestation (vendor-signed), tamper evidence, and deterministic key-wrap vectors. also need a process for handling export-control / crypto licensing questions — legal needs to review.
Sanjay Rao: for the marketplace side, we need metadata for listings: partner logo, support contact, allowed regions, SLA tier, and whether BYOK is supported. attribution: use a referral_code param in checkout + an API webhook to reconcile deals. keeps billing ops simple.
Elena Park: good. on SCIM / SSO: partners must support delta SCIM sync for large orgs (not full re-sync every 24h) and both IdP-initiated and SP-initiated SAML flows for customers that have strict IdP constraints. can we add IdP metadata exchange as a step?
Mateo Alvarez: yes — we'll add a small tool for exchanging SAML metadata (signed XML) and an example `saml-init.sh` script. also include a `scim-delta-sim.py` script for partners to exercise provisioning edge cases.
Claire Dubois: telemetry: require partners to emit these fields in the sandbox report: `keywrap_p95_ms`, `error_rate_5m`, and `provisioning_latency_p95_ms`. we'll provide a dashboard template for the marketplace listing and the acceptance criteria for the validation lab.
Sanjay Rao: commercial ask: we want a simple revenue attribution banding — 90/10 for referrals (low-touch SaaS), 70/30 for SI-led services, and a negotiated co-sell split for enterprise where both teams bring pipeline. also propose $10k co-marketing credit for approved partners that pass the lab. open to feedback.
Elena Park: timeline proposal: publish lab + scripts by next Tue; partners can self-enroll into the sandbox with rate-limited keys; validation window is 2 weeks for turning around tests. after lab pass, we issue a "Validated Connector" badge and start a marketplace listing review (5 business days).
Mateo Alvarez: technical note — require partners to support PKCS#11 slot enumeration and a test vector like:
```
# sample test (pseudo)
openssl rsautl -inkey wrapped.key -decrypt -in secret.bin -out out.bin
# expect fixed 32-byte output for vector 3
```
Sanjay Rao: I'll draft the partner-facing page with listing requirements, timeline, and the referral_code instructions. will include a short FAQ: "what if we only support cloud KMS?" — answer: ok, mark BYOK-cloud only and note restricted regions.
Claire Dubois: legal/compliance: we need to confirm the data processor roles and a minimal DPA addendum for partners that receive customer audit logs. marking legal to review, but recommend we require DPA signed before public listing.
marketplace-bot: created PR #624: partners/marketplace/listings/kms-hsm-validation-lab.md — https://git.redwood.co/pr/624
Elena Park: linking PR. final asks: (1) Mateo — add the `saml-init.sh` and `scim-delta-sim.py` to the repo; (2) Claire — attach the telemetry dashboard JSON + acceptance thresholds; (3) Sanjay — publish the partner page draft and include the attribution webhook schema. target internal review Fri, partner preview next Wed, public listing the following Mon.
Mateo Alvarez: will add scripts today.
Claire Dubois: I'll draft telemetry + attach export-control note for legal.
Sanjay Rao: drafting partner page now. will ping legal about DPA.
Elena Park: thanks all — I'll follow up with a short calendar slot for sign-off and add an "embargo window" checkbox to the partner tracker (48h minimum). :white_check_mark:

title: #partnerships: 3479000000
preview:
Evan Cruz: Quick thread for EdgeScale x Redwood mini-lab (conference booth + livestream) on 2024-06-11. Need AV checklist, accessibility captions, on-site contact, and battery/shipping restrictions.
Priya Natarajan: I'll own captions/CART vendor. Recommend Captionly (we used them at last summit). Need transcript SLA of <=2 min.
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): Logistics: we have 4 demo rigs but 2 have battery packs with lithium cells -> customs + airline paperwork required. Can EdgeScale ops ship freight?
Hannah Cho: Ops note: batteries trigger hazardous paperwork. If partner ships, we need HS codes + MSDS by 2024-05-20. Otherwise we can swap to demo units with external AC only.
Evan Cruz: Could we do a hybrid approach: 2 battery units on-site, 2 hosted fallbacks? That reduces risk.
Priya Natarajan: Accessibility run-of-show suggestion: add 3x 30s caption checks and 1 pause for alt-text descriptions for demo visuals.
ops-bot: Created checklist: "Demo AV + Accessibility" and invited Evan, Priya, Hannah, Leo.
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): On-site lead is Raj Patel -> +1 415-555-0199. He can clear customs on pickup. I'll DM his passport number to Hannah offline.
Hannah Cho: Shipping cutoff for booth arrival (on-site consolidation) is 2024-06-04. Last pickup to meet that = 2024-05-31.
Evan Cruz: Run-of-show (draft):
```
09:30 - Booth open / warmup
10:00 - Stream on / host welcome (Evan)
10:05 - Partner overview (Leo)
10:12 - Demo A: Live inference (EdgeScale hw) - captions ON
10:25 - Demo B: Hosted fallback + policy switch (Priya)
10:35 - 10:40 - Accessibility pause / image descriptions
10:40 - Q&A (moderated)
10:55 - CTA / workshop signup
```
Priya Natarajan: AV needs: 2x HDMI capture (1080p30 ok), 1x camera for close-up, wired ethernet (no Wi-Fi dependency), 2x headset mics. Also a small lav for partner speaker.
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): We'll bring an EdgeScale HDMI breakout and a USB-C power brick. Any venue power strips?
Hannah Cho: Venue supplies standard strips but bring an extra 2m IEC extension. Also bring a travel UPS for the host laptop.
Evan Cruz: Recording: volunteer for single-cam recording? I can handle recording + upload to shared drive. Need someone to clip the 30s highlight for socials.
Priya Natarajan: I'll take clipping. Also will coordinate with Captionly for pre-event test at 2024-06-07 11:00 PT. Caption vendor needs the RTMP stream key by 2024-06-05.
ops-bot: Reminder set: "Submit RTMP key to Captionly" on 2024-06-05.
Hannah Cho: Swag plan: small tote + sticker sheet. Return logistics: include prepaid return label if attendee signs up for post-event kit. Who owns returns?
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): EdgeScale can handle returns for demo loaners. For swag returns we prefer Redwood manage.
Evan Cruz: Action items:
- Leo: send HS codes + MSDS for battery units (due 2024-05-20)
- Hannah: confirm freight cutoff + pickup window (due 2024-05-22)
- Priya: Captionly booking + test stream (due 2024-06-07)
- Evan: upload recording + create highlight clip (due 2024-06-12)
- All: speaker bio (100-150 words) + headshot (png) to marketing by 2024-05-24
Priya Natarajan: Registration cap recommendation: 300 live seats and unlimited on-demand. Keeps booth throughput manageable.
Leo Tan (EdgeScale): Quick fallback plan: if battery units are held at customs, switch to hosted Redwood nodes and show the automap route in console. I can prepare a 2-min recorded fallback clip as Plan B.
Hannah Cho: For customs-friendly shipping, include description: "demo units - no sale value - batteries declared" and add return label. Also include a short packing checklist on the outside of each crate.
Evan Cruz: Great. Also remind speakers to enable live captions on their laptops as a backup (macOS auto-caption or Chrome live captions).
Priya Natarajan: Noted. I'll send a speaker accessibility brief with tips (alt text, speaking cadence, live captions) by 2024-05-28.
Evan Cruz: Thanks team. I'll update the shared doc with these actions and ping again after freight confirmation. :rocket:

title: #partnerships: 3351236789
preview:
Ava Chen: quick sync — ACME SI asked for our SOC report + pen window for marketplace listing. Can we confirm what we can share and dates?
Ravi Patel: we can share SOC2 Type II summary + control mappings. Full report needs an NDA and legal OK.
Maya Ortiz: ACME's PM asked for a pen-test window in mid-May, 2 weeks. Is that feasible from infra?
Liam O'Connor: NDA yes required for the full SOC. For the summary and attestation letter we can share a redacted PDF. I can prep a standard NDA template.
Jordan Mills: from HW partner side: we'll run our side-by-side pen as well. Need presigned S3 links for evidence upload and a retention TTL. 30 days post-upload ok?
Priya Rao: marketplace ops requires artifacts be presigned and tagged with listing-id. TTL usually 90 days for marketplace review, can we stretch to 90?
Ravi Patel: security preference: keep sensitive artifacts behind 7-day presigned links, store uploads in our secure bucket with minimal access. 90-day retention ok in a separate archive if needed.
Ava Chen: action items: 1) legal NDA for full reports (Liam) 2) decide presign TTL and folder layout (Jordan + Ravi) 3) confirm pen-window dates (Maya + Jordan) 4) marketplace artifact tags and owner (Priya).
Maya Ortiz: proposing pen window 2026-05-12 -> 2026-05-26, weekday ops only. Anyone blocked?
Jordan Mills: that window works for our test lab. Need to coordinate blackout for major deploys — eng-releases, please note.
Ravi Patel: can we limit scope to listing endpoints + auth flows? Full infra scans might be overkill for their SI. Scope doc -> I'll paste quick snippet:
```
Scope: marketplace listing endpoints, OAuth flows, file upload ingestion, presign endpoints, admin UI. Exclude: internal infra mgmt, CI runners.
```
Liam O'Connor: NDA and data processing addendum (DPA) draft incoming by EOD tomorrow.
Priya Rao: marketplace metadata: tag format `listing:acme-v1;artifact:pen;owner:partnerships`. I'll put a checklist in the marketplace tracker.
SecurityBot: [Automated] Reminder: schedule a canary rollback window for any listing changes during pen test.
Ava Chen: who owns the response to ACME's security questionnaire (50 Qs)? We should split — security handles the technical Qs, partnerships handles business/process Qs.
Ravi Patel: I'll take technical Qs + evidence pointers. Need list of sample prompts they use for live testing.
Maya Ortiz: I'll coordinate with ACME PM to ask for the exact test vectors and expected endpoints.
Jordan Mills: for evidence upload, we'll provide an S3 prefix `acme/partner-evidence/3351236789/` and a short README. Is that acceptable?
Ravi Patel: yes but make sure ACLs are tight. Use KMS key `redwood-marketplace-evidence` to encrypt.
Liam O'Connor: once DPA/NDA signed, we can share the unredacted SOC under that prefix. Until then, share redacted summary via the marketplace ticket.
Ava Chen: timeline recap: NDA by EOD tomorrow, presign + prefix + TTL confirmed by Jordan/Ravi by Fri, questionnaire split assigned today, pen window mid-May. I'll send a calendar invite after Liam confirms NDA ETA.
Priya Rao: I'll also flag to marketplace review team to prioritize ACME listing during the pen window so they can ingest artifacts quickly.
Maya Ortiz: one more — coordinate joint announcement plan: we should not announce listing until pen + review complete. I'll draft a short comms checklist.
Ravi Patel: and a post-test regression check to validate any hotfixes. Add to runbook.

Ravi Patel: we can share SOC2 Type II summary + control mappings. Full report needs an NDA and legal OK.
Maya Ortiz: ACME's PM asked for a pen-test window in mid-May, 2 weeks. Is that feasible from infra?
Liam O'Connor: NDA yes required for the full SOC. For the summary and attestation letter we can share a redacted PDF. I can prep a standard NDA template.
Jordan Mills: from HW partner side: we'll run our side-by-side pen as well. Need presigned S3 links for evidence upload and a retention TTL. 30 days post-upload ok?
Priya Rao: marketplace ops requires artifacts be presigned and tagged with listing-id. TTL usually 90 days for marketplace review, can we stretch to 90?
Ravi Patel: security preference: keep sensitive artifacts behind 7-day presigned links, store uploads in our secure bucket with minimal access. 90-day retention ok in a separate archive if needed.
Ava Chen: action items: 1) legal NDA for full reports (Liam) 2) decide presign TTL and folder layout (Jordan + Ravi) 3) confirm pen-window dates (Maya + Jordan) 4) marketplace artifact tags and owner (Priya).
Maya Ortiz: proposing pen window 2026-05-12 -> 2026-05-26, weekday ops only. Anyone blocked?
Jordan Mills: that window works for our test lab. Need to coordinate blackout for major deploys — eng-releases, please note.
Ravi Patel: can we limit scope to listing endpoints + auth flows? Full infra scans might be overkill for their SI. Scope doc -> I'll paste quick snippet:
```
Scope: marketplace listing endpoints, OAuth flows, file upload ingestion, presign endpoints, admin UI. Exclude: internal infra mgmt, CI runners.
```
Liam O'Connor: NDA and data processing addendum (DPA) draft incoming by EOD tomorrow.
Priya Rao: marketplace metadata: tag format `listing:acme-v1;artifact:pen;owner:partnerships`. I'll put a checklist in the marketplace tracker.
SecurityBot: [Automated] Reminder: schedule a canary rollback window for any listing changes during pen test.
Ava Chen: who owns the response to ACME's security questionnaire (50 Qs)? We should split — security handles the technical Qs, partnerships handles business/process Qs.
Ravi Patel: I'll take technical Qs + evidence pointers. Need list of sample prompts they use for live testing.
Maya Ortiz: I'll coordinate with ACME PM to ask for the exact test vectors and expected endpoints.
Jordan Mills: for evidence upload, we'll provide an S3 prefix `acme/partner-evidence/3351236789/` and a short README. Is that acceptable?
Ravi Patel: yes but make sure ACLs are tight. Use KMS key `redwood-marketplace-evidence` to encrypt.
Liam O'Connor: once DPA/NDA signed, we can share the unredacted SOC under that prefix. Until then, share redacted summary via the marketplace ticket.
Ava Chen: timeline recap: NDA by EOD tomorrow, presign + prefix + TTL confirmed by Jordan/Ravi by Fri, questionnaire split assigned today, pen window mid-May. I'll send a calendar invite after Liam confirms NDA ETA.
Priya Rao: I'll also flag to marketplace review team to prioritize ACME listing during the pen window so they can ingest artifacts quickly.
Maya Ortiz: one more — coordinate joint announcement plan: we should not announce listing until pen + review complete. I'll draft a short comms checklist.
Ravi Patel: and a post-test regression check to validate any hotfixes. Add to runbook.
Ava Chen: perfect. thanks all. tagging next steps: @Liam O'Connor (NDA), @Jordan Mills (presign & prefix), @Ravi Patel (scope + tech Qs), @Priya Rao (marketplace tag + prioritization), @Maya Ortiz (PM coordination).
Jordan Mills: noted, will update when presigns available.
Liam O'Connor: NDA sent to ACME contact.
Ravi Patel: scope doc uploaded to internal drive, link: https://internal.redwood/drive/abc123 (restricted).
Ava Chen: closing this thread — will reopen if dates shift. :thumbsup:

title: #partnerships: 3166000000
preview:
Jordan Blake: Quick thread 1: Partner wants a structured private-offer waterfall (commit bands + step-down renewals) and expects to be able to on-board SI activation early. Ops/legal/finance need rules.
Mateo Ruiz: TL;DR: we can support tiered discounts but need fixed metadata for marketplace. also partner requests automatic migration credit if customer converts within 60 days.
Sam (Legal): Must include explicit renewal waterfall in the private-offer terms. if it's not codified, renewals become ad-hoc. sample language: "Year1: X% off; Year2: Y% off; Year3: Z% off (automatic step-down unless renegotiated)". Also need confidentiality clause until listing.
Aisha (Finance): Credits = contra-revenue per accounting. we require a hard cap per customer and global ceiling for exposure. propose cap = min($125k, 12% of committed ARR). will model sensitivity.
Priya Singh: Partner insists on early SI activation to demo to key accounts. ops question: do we allow usage to count toward the committed bucket pre-list?
Mateo Ruiz: We can allow it but only if partner provides signed SOW and we issue a staging SKU mapping token. record all usage and reconcile post-list.
Sam (Legal): Add appendix: SKU mapping + 60-day deprecation notice. include audit rights for usage reconciliation.
Jordan Blake: Marketing embargo: no partner-led public mentions until the marketplace listing is live. partners may share private previews under NDA.
market-bot: Created checklist: marketplace-waterfall/3166000000. Reminder: attach private-offer T&Cs to listing metadata.
Aisha (Finance): Finance needs clarity on how marketplace fee affects net economics (marketplace take). include an example calculation in the private-offer docs so partners understand net price to customer vs ARR impact.
Mateo Ruiz: Ops to-do: implement temporary mapping token flow:
```
1) partner submits mapping CSV
2) eng-ops issues temp SKU token for activation
3) usage logs tagged with token
4) on listing, swap token -> marketplace SKU and emit audit event
5) run reconciliation and apply credits if conversion criteria met
```
Sam (Legal): For migration credits: cap them, treat as contra-revenue, and include an expiry (60d). also require partner to confirm automation for credit application (webhook or file feed).
Priya Singh: SI enablement ask: 30-day window to complete runbook after the soft-list. partner wants access to docs earlier; we can provide a redacted runbook before sign-off.
Jordan Blake: Decision proposal:
- Allow early activation with temp SKU token if SOW signed
- Count pre-list usage toward committed spend but require monthly reconciliation and audit rights
- Allow one-time migration credit on conversion (60d expiry) with finance cap
- Include explicit 3-year renewal waterfall in T&Cs
- Add appendix for SKU mapping and 60-day deprecation notice
- Marketing embargo until listing live; private previews allowed via NDA
Aisha (Finance): I'll produce the cap recommendation + P&L scenarios by EOD.
Sam (Legal): I'll draft the private-offer T&Cs + appendix and circulate redlines tomorrow morning.
Mateo Ruiz: I'll produce the token flow spec and a sample mapping CSV.
market-bot: Reminder scheduled: cross-functional sign-off meeting 3166086400 (24h).
Jordan Blake: Thanks everyone 1: let's keep the partner happy but protect revenue and compliance. reconvene tomorrow at 9am PT.

title: #partnerships: 3131111111
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync: EdgeForge wants to ship a signed upload flow for device-metering to reduce reconciliation noise. They9ll push gzipped CSVs to our S3 intake and POST a manifest + sig to our webhook.
Diego Morales: That sounds fine. We need an explicit hash algorithm (sha256) and a manifest format. Also agree on timezones and clock skew handling for device timestamps.
Priya Shah: Requirements from finance from last quarter: we must be able to run a reconciliation delta report per SKU and per customer daily, and mark any >1% variance as a P1 for manual review.
Roberto Lin: Proposed manifest (example):
```
{
  "files": [
    {"name":"usage-2026-03-18.gz","sha256":"<hex>","rows":43210}
  ],
  "partner_id":"edgeforge-001",
  "generated_at":"2026-03-18T08:00:00Z"
}
```
Signature is over the raw manifest bytes.
Ava Chen: CSV header we expect: `event_id,timestamp_iso,device_id,customer_id,partner_sku,units,currency`
Diego Morales: Important: device timestamps are local (tz offset present). We will normalize to UTC on ingest and store both original and normalized for audit. Reject if parsed timestamp is > 10m in future.
Priya Shah: Backfill rules: accept backfill up to 120 days if partner includes `backfill_reason` and files are flagged `is_backfill=true`. Otherwise only 30 days. Finance wants an automated flag in the reconciliation indicating which rows were backfilled.
Roberto Lin: Dedup semantics: `event_id` is the canonical dedupe key. We keep dedupe index TTL = 45 days for bulk flow. For realtime events we keep 7 days.
Ava Chen: Reconciliation flow notes:
- Ingest transforms partner_sku -> marketplace_sku via `partner_sku_map`
- Aggregate hourly and daily totals per marketplace_sku
- Compare partner-reported totals vs computed totals; produce `delta_pct`
- Any `delta_pct` > 1% at daily granularity triggers `recon_alert`.
MeterBot: Automated check: incoming manifest will be validated against `schema:partner-manifest-v1`; failures will post a short report to #partnerships-recon.
Diego Morales: Error samples for partner debugging:
```
HTTP/1.1 400 Bad Request
{ "error":"bad_manifest","detail":"missing files array" }
```
```
HTTP/1.1 422 Unprocessable Entity
{ "error":"delta_too_large","detail":"daily delta 4.2% for sku redwood:dedicated:gpu.premium.hour" }
```
Priya Shah: For auditability we need: original manifest + sig, parsed row samples (first/last 5), ingestion log (sha256, rowcount), and reconciliation summary attached to the daily report. Retain artifacts for 365d.
Roberto Lin: Replay handling: if we detect out-of-order batches (older watermark) we run a replay job that marks affected aggregates as `recomputed` and annotate with `replay_id` for auditors.
Ava Chen: Partner contract question: do we accept compressed JSONL or only CSV? EdgeForge prefers CSV. We can add JSONL later but document now: CSV only.
Diego Morales: Security: partner public keys stored in `partner_keys` with rotation policy. We verify sig using partner public key and allow `ed25519` or `rsa-sha256`. For HMAC flows use a separate endpoint.
Priya Shah: Action items:
- @Diego: write manifest + signature verification spec and example responses (PR by Mon)
- @Roberto: add dedupe TTL and replay_id design to ingest-runbook (RFC by Tue)
- @Ava: coordinate with EdgeForge to get a sample gzipped CSV + manifest for dry-run next week
- @MeterBot: post automated dry-run results to #partnerships-recon and tag owners when delta > 0.5%
Diego Morales: I'll also add a small CLI snippet partners can use to sign manifests (node + go examples).
Ava Chen: Target: dry-run with sample data Wednesday. If we can run reconciliation and confirm delta < 0.5% we move to gated production. Ping me if anything blocks. :white_check_mark:

title: #partnerships: 2839990011
preview:
Maya Chen: FYI AcmeCloud PO #AC-2024-987 is blocked in the marketplace portal — portal rejects the invoice due to 'legal name/address mismatch'. Can procurement + legal take a look?
Raj Patel: Ugh. Is this the merchant alias vs legal entity issue again?
Maya Chen: Looks like marketplace expects 'AcmeCloud, Inc.' but the invoice uses 'AcmeCloud LLC' (they recently re-org'd). portal error: 'ENTITY_ID_NORMALIZATION_FAILED'.
Priya Singh: @Maya can you paste the raw payload that hit the portal? marketplace ops might need the exact field set.
Maya Chen: Here — payload snippet from PO system:
```json
{"po_id":"AC-2024-987","vendor_id":"ACME-0001","billing_entity":"AcmeCloud LLC","address":"1234 Cloud Way, Suite 200, Denver, CO 80202","tax_id":"12-3456789"}
```
Elena Gomez: We can't book the invoice until legal entity matches our vendor ledger. Finance can temporarily hold under 'AP-HOLD: vendor_alias' if legal signs a new W-9, but that's manual.
Tom Rivers: Need executed MSA amendment or updated W-9/EIN docs from the vendor. Without that we can't change ledger mapping.
AcmeCloud Sales: Sorry — we've been onboarding a new legal entity. I'll upload an updated W-9 and signed MSA amendment by EOD and ask marketplace to accept a merchant alias. Apologies for the churn.
Jordan Blake: If marketplace won't accept alias, we can add a temporary mapping in our ledger service to map 'AcmeCloud LLC' -> 'AcmeCloud, Inc.' but that requires a ticket and an audit trail. Example API call we used before:
```bash
curl -X POST https://internal-ledger.redwood/api/mappings \ 
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer $LEDGER_KEY' \ 
  -d '{"external_name":"AcmeCloud LLC","canonical_name":"AcmeCloud, Inc.","reason":"marketplace alias temporary mapping"}'
```
Priya Singh: Marketplace support sometimes pushes back on manual mappings. I'll open a support case (ref: MSP-92431) and ask for an alias accept.
deploy-bot: PO ingestion failed for AC-2024-987 — reason: ENTITY_ID_NORMALIZATION_FAILED (ingest-id: igst-283999-12)
Raj Patel: If marketplace support is slow, can finance accept under the legacy vendor code + create a reconciliation ticket? What's the customer impact (billing/delivery)?
Elena Gomez: Customer-facing: none yet. We haven't invoiced. Risk: delayed payment to vendor and blocked deployment of reserved marketplace credits.
Tom Rivers: Legal: we can provisionally approve a mapping if vendor provides a scanned W-9 and the signed amendment. Need both to clear compliance.
AcmeCloud Sales: Uploading docs now: https://acmecloud.share/docs/w9_acmecloud.pdf and MSA_amendment_signed.pdf — please confirm receipts.
Maya Chen: Received links. @Priya please escalate MSP-92431 with attachments and ask for manual accept. @Jordan please standby for ledger mapping only after Legal confirms docs.
Priya Singh: Escalation created, pinged rep, and included uploaded docs. Marketplace replied: 'Need proof of EIN match + original PO signer info.' They want a notarized EIN affidavit for alias change (ugh).
Tom Rivers: Notarized affidavit is heavy — ask vendor if they can provide company bank statement + EIN confirmation letter from IRS (sufficient in many cases).
AcmeCloud Sales: Can provide bank confirmation + EIN letter. Will push notarized affidavit if required.
Jordan Blake: Small ops note — if we add a temporary mapping we should tag it with 'ttl:7d' and create a reconciler ticket to revert mapping after close. I can populate the ticket template.
Maya Chen: Action items: 1) Acme uploads bank + EIN letter (AcmeCloud). 2) Legal (Tom) to review docs ASAP. 3) Priya to press marketplace for alias accept and manual PO clear. 4) Jordan to prepare ledger mapping template but not apply until legal OK. 5) Finance (Elena) to prepare AP hold fallback.
Raj Patel: Thanks — let me know if I should loop in sales leadership for further escalation.
AcmeCloud Sales: We'll prioritize docs. ETA 3pm PT.
Tom Rivers: I'll review by 6pm PT and post checklist here.
Maya Chen: Great — marking this as blocking for the deal desk. Will follow up in 2 hours. :hourglass_flowing_sand:
Priya Singh: Marketplace ticket updated with attachments and escalated to partner support engineer. (MSP-92431 -> level2).
Elena Gomez: Finance set AP-HOLD (internal ref: APH-7782) as contingency.
Maya Chen: Thanks everyone. Next update when legal confirms or marketplace accepts.

title: #partnerships: 2770012345
preview:
Tess Alvarez: Quick ping 1 Nimbus/Skyline conversation. They sent a draft MOU requesting us to host their model + allow them to distribute a 'lite' quantized bundle to SI partners. Need us to confirm redistribution rights and logo usage.
Noah Kim: Can we get the exact license file? If it's permissive we can consider downloadable artifacts, otherwise hosted-only.
Priya Desai: Product note: downloadable bundles mean extra SKUs and support SLAs. Also need an explicit QA gate for quantized variants.
Ben Carter: Sales: don't accept a single-revenue-split across all channels. Propose tiered splits per SKU and a minimum revenue guarantee for the first 90 days.
Tess Alvarez: Legal asked for export control check if weights are downloadable to EEA/APAC. Also trademark use guidelines.
Noah Kim: Eng ops: if downloads allowed we need artifact signing, a provenance record (hash), and a compatibility matrix (runtime versions). Also CI evals for hallucination/regression.
Priya Desai: Marketing: propose a joint announce plan with a 10-business-day embargo and a single liaison from Skyline for creative approvals. Need co-branding mockups by Mon.
Ben Carter: Commercial counter: per-SKU splits + 3-month review clause + right to convert to hosted-only if misuse detected. I'll draft and share.
marketplace-bot: Template reminder: partner-mou-template v2.0 at https://redwood.internal/partner-templates.
Tess Alvarez: Action list: 1) ask Skyline for license file and brand pack, 2) legal to do license/export check, 3) eng to spec quant gate + eval set, 4) sales to prepare counter. Deadline: close internal feedback by Thu EOD.

Noah Kim: Can we get the exact license file? If it's permissive we can consider downloadable artifacts, otherwise hosted-only.

marketplace-bot: Template reminder: partner-mou-template v2.0 at https://redwood.internal/partner-templates.

title: #partnerships: 2539001123
preview:
Ethan Park: Morning—scoped a new risk. Vendor reported a firmware hold on SKU rtx-a1-rack batch B-19. QC flagged a minor ROM mismatch; vendor paused shipment MNL-2330. Affects RES-99500 (Private EU) and DED-7401.
Noelle Briggs: How many racks impacted?
Orion Logistics Rep: About 10 racks in that container. QC wants vendor to reflash before export. They estimate 72-96h.
Ana Silva: Private customer needs 4 racks for canary, Dedicated has a buffer for 2 days.
Victor Huang: Can we source replacement racks from APAC staging? We have APAC pool AP-881 with 12 available but cross-shipping timeline ~7 days.
Ethan Park: Two mitigation options: (1) Accept 72-96h firmware reflash and shift canary schedule (2) Cross-ship 4 racks from AP-881 to EU (7 days) + move 2 from DED buffer.
Noelle Briggs: What about partner announcement timeline? They confirmed a joint blog next Wed. If we miss canary we can't sign-off.
Ana Silva: Could we dual-run the announcement with limited availability note? Risky. Also legal may need to approve messaging.
PartnerOpsBot: Suggested action: create ticket CS-9410 (firmware-hold) and notify Legal & PR.
Orion Logistics Rep: Vendor asked to prioritize reflash for the 4 canary racks; they can be ready in ~36h if we confirm priority.
Victor Huang: Ok prioritize reflash for 4 racks. We'll take 2 from Dedicated buffer and request 2 from vendor-priority. That should keep canary on track with small schedule slip.
Ethan Park: Confirming: vendor will reflash 4 priority units in 36h, we draw 2 from DED buffer now. PartnerOpsBot: CS-9410 created and assigned to Ethan and Orion.
Noelle Briggs: I'll draft the partner message: "small, scheduled hardware verification causing minor ship window shift—we expect canary unchanged; will update if anything changes." Keep it optimistic.
Ana Silva: I'll loop Legal & PR once Noelle posts draft.
Orion Logistics Rep: Shipping update to follow; well mark priority units on the manifest.
Ethan Park: Thanks team. Keep this thread tight; we'll escalate if vendor pushes back.

title: #partnerships: 2391223333
preview:
Rohit Singh: Heads-up: prepping the partner ops section for next week's QBR. Need to nail tier movement triggers + MDF emergency rules.
Taylor Nguyen: Ok — propose we codify three movement triggers: 1) Upgrade: partner-sourced ARR > $140k over trailing 90 days AND gen pipeline conversion > 2%. 2) Downgrade: < 35% of committed pipeline closed (or formally stalled) for two quarters. 3) Fast-track upgrade (for launches): forecasted incremental ARR > $200k within 6 months with partner exec sponsor.
Marta Lopez: For downgrades, let's not penalize deals stalled by the customer's procurement/legal delays. Tag `procurement-delay` or `legal-hold` to exclude.
Taylor Nguyen: Agree. Add a 45-day grace window post-product-launch.
Jules Martinez: MDF caps? Finance wants fixed ceilings. My draft: Tier A $180k/yr, Tier B $60k/yr, Tier C $15k/yr. Emergency top-up of +40% one-time with approvals.
Ava Chen: Can we require a simple ROI one-pager for emergency funds (expected pipeline, key milestones, break-even estimate)? Also 3 partner-provided references for co-sell credibility.
Rohit Singh: Yes. Emergency committee = Partnerships + Finance + Product (asynchronous review within 72 hours). Approval conditioned on ROI memo + signed co-marketing plan.
Taylor Nguyen: Operational rules to include: - escape-hatch funds must be spent within 90 days or returned - unique cost-center tag on all spend - monthly spend report posted to #partnerships - finance CSV template for ledger.
partners-bot: FYI QBR packet template updated: https://redwood-infer.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/partnerships/pages/qbr-packet-template (auto-post)
Jules Martinez: Sample finance CSV I mocked below:
```
partner,tier,annual_commitment,mdf_allocated,cost_center
CloudRange,TierA,750000,150000,PC-4201
SysIntegrateX,TierB,300000,60000,PC-4302
EdgeWorks,TierC,90000,15000,PC-4403
```
Marta Lopez: Also propose a simple FAQ for partners explaining triggers and fairness process (appeal window, data used, exception handling).
Ava Chen: Partner readiness checklist for emergency requests: 1) Joint GTM plan 2) Lead routing matrix 3) 3 refs 4) Expected close timeline 5) Co-marketing assets 6) Commitment to monthly reporting.
Taylor Nguyen: Action items: Rohit/Taylor finalize thresholds + FAQ (due Wed EOD), Jules format CSV + ledger example (due Thu), Marta define cost center naming + reporting template (due Thu), Ava produce the partner checklist (due Wed).
Rohit Singh: One more operational note: downgrade notifications should be auto-generated and include remediation steps (enablement, demand gen support). Don't want surprises.
Taylor Nguyen: Good point. I'll add a remediation 30/60/90 plan template to the QBR packet.
Jules Martinez: Also include a short blurb for the customer success teams about handling partner escalations during downgrades.
Marta Lopez: Agreed. We should also log all escape-hatch approvals in a shared spreadsheet with timestamps and ROI memo link.
Taylor Nguyen: I'll upload a draft into the QBR folder before tomorrow's prep sync 9:30am PT. If anyone objects to thresholds, flag now or bring to the sync. :thumbsup:
Ava Chen: Thanks — this is actionable.
Rohit Singh: Thanks team.

title: #partnerships: 2391110000
preview:
Taylor Nguyen: Heads up — prepping the Q partner review next week. I want us to finalize the trigger mechanics we'll present: when a partner moves between tiers, and the emergency co-invest (aka escape hatch) rules for MDF spend.
Rohit Singh: 👍 Can you summarise the proposed triggers? Numbers help.
Taylor Nguyen: Short version: 1) Tier upgrade if partner ARR sourced > $150k over trailing 90 days and lead gen conversion > 2.5%. 2) Tier downgrade if partner activity < 30% of their committed pipeline for two consecutive quarters. 3) Emergency co-invest unlock when a partner hits a one-time product-market event (launch, large SI deal) and forecasts > $250k incremental ARR in 6 months.
Jules Martinez: Those look reasonable. For downgrade rule: do we count paused deals? sometimes opportunities sit in pipeline but stall due to customer procurement.
Ava Chen: Good point — propose we exclude deals marked `procurement-delay` or `legal-block` for downgrade calc. Otherwise we punish partners for customer-side slowdowns.
Taylor Nguyen: Agreed. Add clause: exclude deals tagged with `procurement-delay` or `legal-block`. Also recommend a 30-day grace after major product launches.
Marta Lopez: On MDF: suggest a 3-tier co-invest cap relative to partner tier: Tier 1 max $200k/year, Tier 2 $75k/year, Tier 3 $20k/year. Emergency escape hatch can add one-time +50% on top of cap with VP approval.
Rohit Singh: That escape hatch number seems high for Tier 1. Can we gate it by ROI projection and an emergency committee (reps from partnerships, finance, product)?
Taylor Nguyen: Yes — require a 2-page ROI memo + timeline and signatures from Partnerships PM, Finance lead, and Product lead. Emergency committee meets asynchronously within 48 hours.
partners-bot: Reminder: QBR packet template updated: https://redwood-infer.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/partnerships/pages/qbr-packet-template (auto-post)
Jules Martinez: FYI finance asked for a sample budget line so they can wire the MDF pool. Minimal CSV I mocked:
```
partner,tier,annual_commitment,mdf_allocated
CloudRange,Tier1,600000,150000
SysIntegrateX,Tier2,250000,50000
EdgeWorks,Tier3,80000,10000
```
Ava Chen: Can we add a short checklist for the partner before they request the escape hatch? e.g., co-marketing plan, co-selling motion, lead routing agreement, 3 reference customers.
Taylor Nguyen: Yes — I'll draft a 6-point checklist and circulate. Also propose we add a "sunset" condition: escape-hatch funds must be spent within 90 days of approval or return to MDF pool.
Marta Lopez: Noted. Also: tagging and reporting — every escape-hatch approval needs a unique cost center tag and a monthly spend report posted to the partnerships channel.
Rohit Singh: Action items: 1) Taylor: draft final trigger rules + checklist (due EOD Thu). 2) Jules: format CSV for finance + a sample journal entry (due Fri). 3) Ava: quick partner readiness checklist (due Thu). 4) Marta: define cost center tagging and reporting template (due Fri).
Taylor Nguyen: I'll run this at our prep sync tomorrow 10am PT. If anyone has objections to the thresholds, speak now or bring to the sync. :thumbsup:
Jules Martinez: One more ask — include a small FAQ for partners explaining why we have downgrade triggers; want to keep it transparent and avoid surprises.
Taylor Nguyen: Good call. I'll add an FAQ section in the QBR packet.
Ava Chen: Thanks all — this feels actionable.
Marta Lopez: +1, thanks.
Taylor Nguyen: Closing the loop: sync tomorrow 10am PT, I'll post the draft in #partnerships before then.

title: #partnerships: 2246154321
preview:
Ava Chen: Hey team — it's T-10 days for the CloudMarket launch with NexusCloud. Quick checklist: press embargo, joint blog draft, landing page final copy, tracking pixel, legal signoff. Who owns what?
Diego Martinez: I'll own the landing page updates and pixel. Will push draft to staging by EOD tomorrow.
Priya Raj: I'm on the joint blog with Nexus comms. Draft v0.3 in Google Drive: https://drive.google.com/xxx. Need Nexus quote by Wed.
Liam O'Connor: release-bot shows code freeze scheduled 2026-04-18. Can we confirm integration test batch for marketplace listing?
release-bot: Reminder: code-freeze window starts in 9 days. See /releases
Ava Chen: Legal signoff is pending — @Priya can you ping Nexus legal and @Diego can you drop the TOS snippet into the page? Ideally have legal ack by Thu.
Diego Martinez: Yep will add TOS. Also need product screenshots; @Liam can you grab the latest from QA?
Liam O'Connor: On it. Will export high-res images and upload to the shared folder by tomorrow morning.
Priya Raj: Nexus has one ask: can we include a joint quote and link to their partner page. I'm drafting quote now.
Ava Chen: For press: press list is finalized. @Diego please confirm the embargo timestamp for the press release — Nexus wants 9am PT 4/20.
Diego Martinez: 9am PT 4/20 works for us. Adding embargo meta to the release doc.
Priya Raj: Blog timeline: internal review Mon, Nexus review Tue, final Wed. If Nexus misses quote by Wed noon we'll fallback to a product-focused quote.
Ava Chen: Marketing needs the landing URL, final hero headline, and 3 bullets by Wed EOD for creative assets.
Diego Martinez: Landing URL will be /marketplace/nexuscloud, hero headline proposal: "Redwood + NexusCloud: Fast, predictable LLM inference in your cloud". Thoughts?
Liam O'Connor: Slight tweak: "Predictable LLM inference — now on NexusCloud" (shorter)
Priya Raj: That reads cleaner. Also recommend adding a subhead about SLA-backed capacity.
Ava Chen: Great. Ops question: tracking — @diego remember to add UTM params: utm_source=marketplace&utm_medium=partner&utm_campaign=nexus_launch
Diego Martinez: noted
Priya Raj: Nexus comms asked about embargoed assets (screenshots, logo). Legal asked for logo usage guidelines. @Ava do we have the vector logo ready?
Ava Chen: Yes, uploaded to drive under /assets/partners/nexus. Legal: don't use without trademark line. I'll attach usage doc.
Liam O'Connor: Quick blocker: our staging marketplace listing failed a schema validation yesterday. Raised ticket #MP-453 in support. ETA for fix 48hrs.
Ava Chen: Thanks — note in runbook to switch to backup listing if not fixed by Tue EOD.
Priya Raj: Media outreach: @Ava do we want to offer Nexus a pre-brief with 3 target reporters? I can coordinate with Nexus PR.

title: #partnerships: 2190004321
preview:
Maya Patel: Heads up — Vanguard Compute just returned a countersigned NDA and want to kick off a short early-access bench/ops loop next week. They requested a 1-node sandbox, telemetry access, and a short press embargo.

Diego Ruiz: Great. Do we have a data transfer plan? S3 or SFTP? Also need to confirm marketplace packaging won't block timeline.

Maya Patel: They prefer SFTP for initial drops, will open an S3 endpoint later. They also want a 30-day embargo on any external announcements. @Priya Natarajan can you handle the addendum?

Priya Natarajan: On it. I'll push the NDA addendum today and aim for countersign by EOD tomorrow. Need the Vanguard contact (email) to cc legal.

Connor Lee: For the bench plan, propose we focus on: rwkv-30b, llama2-13b, and a quantized-mid model (q4_8). Test matrix: batch sizes [1,8,32], seq lengths [512,2048], measure p50/p90/p99, throughput and power draw. Also a 2-hour thermal soak run.

Alex Kim: Telemetry: Vanguard will expose GPU power and thermal sensors. We'll ingest into redwood.telemetry.v1. Minimal schema below — we'll need a mapping step on our side:

```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-25T16:01:23Z",
  "gpu_id": "gpu-0",
  "power_watts": 345.2,
  "temp_c": 78.1,
  "kv_cache_hit": 0.987
}
```

Connor Lee: Also want network hop counts and PCIe utilization if available. We will correlate with p99 spikes for regression analysis.

Maya Patel: Ops notes: Vanguard can grant lab access Tue-Fri 09:00-17:00 PT next week and only one node initially. We need to order runs to get the most informative data.

Diego Ruiz: Proposed run order: 
1) Baseline perf rwkv-30b (batch=1, seq=512)
2) Quantized run q4_8 (batch=8, seq=512)
3) Long-seq latency (seq=2048, batch=1)
4) Thermal stress (2hr sustained load)

Priya Natarajan: NDA addendum request: add joint-PR clause — no press/release without mutual signoff; 30-day embargo after shared results. I'll add liability caps aligned with our standard EAP terms.

Maya Patel: Owners: Legal=Priya, Bench infra & scripts=Connor, Telemetry ingestion=Alex, Partner ops liaison=Diego. I'll coordinate daily check-ins. :white_check_mark:

partner-bot: Calendar invite created — "Vanguard bench kickoff" on Wed Mar 25 2026 16:00 PT. Join: https://meet.google.com/xxx (invite sent to participants).

Connor Lee: Quick bench script snippet for infra team:

```bash
# run baseline
./bench/run.sh --model rwkv-30b --batch 1 --seq 512 --iters 200 --out results/rwkv-baseline.json
```

Diego Ruiz: SFTP creds exchanged in DM with Maya. We'll also drop a golden prompt set (50 prompts) for quality parity checks.

ProductOps: Marketplace checklist: we need manifest.json, ops runbook, pricing SKU mapping, and a small deployment example. I’ll prep a template for Vanguard to fill.

Alex Kim: Telemetry notes: Vanguard will rotate keys daily — we'll add automation to fetch new creds and rotate our ingestion. Also adding a lightweight parser to validate incoming fields and drop noisy sensors.

Priya Natarajan: Confirmed vendor rule: no customer data in these runs — Vanguard says all workloads will be synthetic. I'll add that to the addendum.

Maya Patel: Good. Diego will post daily bench summaries in #partnerships/results. If any parity regressions show up, Connor please escalate.

Diego Ruiz: First quick summary (posted to results): baseline p99=42ms, throughput=120 tok/s, power idle ~320W; thermal ramp stable over 30m. Attaching logs to the results thread.

Connor Lee: Noted. I'll triage parity diffs and open follow-ups as needed.

Maya Patel: Thanks everyone — solid kickoff. We'll use this thread for ops updates and any blocker triage. :rocket:

title: #partnerships: 2123456789
preview:
Ethan Park: Heads up 1 ops flagged inconsistent commit floors across vendors. We need SKU-level min_monthly_commit (usd) and min_commit_units per partner so cost modeling doesn't blow up for micro-reserves. Also ask for any fulfillment surcharges tied to cross-region traffic.

Lina Gomez: Reached out to Zenith & Aurora. Quick notes: Zenith says min_commit_units=8 or min_monthly_commit=$12,000; cross_region_egress_pct=0.8% and a fulfillment surcharge = 1.0% + $500 per TB after the first 2TB. Aurora replied: marketplace_fee_pct=3.0% flat, no hard min if you sign a partner MOU but there's a $2k onboarding fee for <5-device pools. Vega replied yesterday with a CSV but flagged variable per-region egress tiers.

Devon Cruz: For the schema, product wants these columns: sku,region,device_type,reserved_1mo,reserved_12mo,reserved_36mo,spot_avg,spot_eviction_pct,min_commit_units,min_monthly_commit_usd,fulfillment_surcharge_pct,per_tb_egress_fee_usd,cross_region_egress_pct,marketplace_fee_pct,billing_cycle_days,rounding_policy,sku_family,fallback_variant_sku

PartnerOpsBot: zenith-sku-fulfillment.csv uploaded by Lina Gomez  link: https://internal.storage/zenith-sku-fulfillment.csv

Ethan Park: Dropping a sanitized CSV snippet from Zenith for reference:

```
sku,region,device_type,reserved_12mo,reserved_36mo,spot_avg,spot_eviction_pct,min_commit_units,min_monthly_commit_usd,fulfillment_surcharge_pct,per_tb_egress_fee_usd,cross_region_egress_pct,marketplace_fee_pct
zn-gpu-h100-80,us-east,nvidia_h100,14.50,12.00,5.80,1.2,8,12000,1.0,500,0.8,2.0
zn-gpu-a100-40,eu-west,nvidia_a100_40,8.90,7.20,3.50,2.9,8,12000,1.0,500,1.1,2.0
au-gpu-h100-80,us-west,nvidia_h100,15.00,13.00,6.00,1.8,5,0,0.0,0,0.6,3.0
```

Sasha Kim: For finance scenarios I'm going to add a "micro-commit penalty" case where reserved pools < min_commit_units incur +15% effective cost and a "high-fulfillment" case where per-TB egress fees spike x3. Also will model marketplace_fee +/- 1.5pp.

Maria Lopez: Ops note: watch VAT/GST and invoicing country. A few partners route payments through subsidiaries which creates VAT on top of marketplace fees and we get short-paid invoices. Also some partners round invoice totals to the nearest $10 which causes reconciliation noise.

Devon Cruz: Can vendors also provide billing_cycle_days and payment_terms_days plus early_pay_discount_pct? That helps us model float and net present value of discounts. Add rounding_policy (\"round_up\",\"round_nearest\",\"no_round\").

Lina Gomez: Zenith SLA credit wording: \"Invoice credit applied when monthly uptime <99% - 5% credit; <97% - 12% credit; credits applied to next invoice, capped at 20% of invoice total.\" Aurora offered either invoice credit or pro-rated refund at customer request.

Ethan Park: Action items: Lina to gather final CSVs from Zenith, Aurora, Vega by Thu COB; Devon to update the canonical CSV template with the fields above; Sasha to run 4 scenarios (baseline, micro-commit penalty, high-fulfillment, high-eviction shock) and share numbers; Maria to compile historical VAT/rounding incidents for legal/finance; I'll consolidate vendor redlines and circulate negotiation asks. :thumbsup:

PartnerOpsBot: uploaded aurora-sku-metadata.csv by Lina Gomez  link: https://internal.storage/aurora-sku-metadata.csv

Sasha Kim: I'll publish the scenario sheet in channel when done. ETA tomorrow morning.

Devon Cruz: I'll add a sample fallback mapping table to the template so routing can simulate fallback_variant_sku -> blended cost.

Ethan Park: If anyone sees partners that insist on unusual payment routing or >$500 onboarding for small pools, drop the examples here so finance/legal can pre-clear.

Lina Gomez: Will do. cc @Devon @Sasha @Maria

title: #partnerships: 1955555555
preview:
Anya Kozlov: Heads-up team — EdgeForum partner workshop (onsite + live stream) in two weeks. Need final list: speaker order, who ships demo loaners, swag inventory for hands-on attendees, and media pickup for partner B-roll.
Marcus Lee: I can lead media pickup and capture B-roll. Will need a list of partner logos and any brand guidelines for on-camera frames.
Sofia Alvarez: I'll own speaker order and queue. Recommend 5 lightning talks (8m each) then 3 partner demos (15m). Suggest partners submit 2-min pre-recorded fallback clips.
Ben Carter: Hardware: packing 4 loaner boxes, each with RL demo rig + power kit. Plan B: if live rig fails, we run recorded benchmark and demo overlay. Need return-label instructions printed inside each box.
Lia Gomez: Swag: 25 attendee bundles (sticker sheet, cable, quick-start card) + 6 VIP partner bundles. I can co-brand the quick-start card — partners to send logos by Thu. Also confirm customs paperwork for Canada (HS codes).
Anya Kozlov: Draft schedule below. Flag any timing issues.
Anya Kozlov: ```
09:00 Load-in & AV check
09:30 Welcome + housekeeping (5m)
09:35 Lightning talks (5 x 8m)
10:15 Coffee break + swag table opens
10:30 Partner demos (3 x 15m) + 5m buffers
11:30 Wrap + post-event survey QR
```
Marcus Lee: Media notes — please send partner stickers and logo assets by Thu EOD. I'll prep lower-third graphics and a short 30s hero clip per partner.
Sofia Alvarez: For speaker handoff, let's have 1 designated laptop operator per partner. Marcus, can you tag someone to monitor B-roll uploads?
Ben Carter: I'll include a short "how to connect" card in the kit: network SSID, static IP config, and contact support@redwood.ai. Lia, can you fold that into the quick-start?
Lia Gomez: Yup. Also adding a QR linking to repo with sample prompts and a short video walkthrough. Partners need to approve co-branding.
ops-bot: Reminder scheduled: dry-run 2026-06-02 09:00 PT. Auto-DM to hosts 45m before.
Anya Kozlov: Logistics decision: Canada & EU partners ship direct; NA partners ship to Redwood HQ for QA. Lia please produce manifest and share PO.
Marcus Lee: Equipment list for media: 1x field recorder, 2x lavs, tripod, HDMI capture. Ben, can you confirm HDMI passthrough on rigs?
Ben Carter: Confirmed HDMI passthrough present. I'll bring spare HDMI encoders and 2 USB-C adapters.
Sofia Alvarez: Recording: attendee stream + partner raw. I'll handle attendee stream. Marcus will archive partner raw. Post-event: delete transient debug logs.
Lia Gomez: Raffle: digital survey entry for swag table. 6 speaker-curated VIP bundles as prizes. Legal review needed for EU giveaway. Tagging legal.
Anya Kozlov: Action list: Sofia — speaker order + operator names by Mon. Lia — swag manifest + co-brand approvals by Tue. Ben — kit packing + spare encoders by Wed. Marcus — media prep + logo assets by Thu. I'll distribute final runbook after rehearsal.
Marcus Lee: I'll upload hero clips to s3://media.redwood/events/edgeforum/ after capture. Reminder to scrub any personal data.
Lia Gomez: Labels printed for NA staging. Shipping scheduled Wed. Will post tracking in #logistics channel.
Anya Kozlov: Thanks team — let's aim to close items by Wednesday EOD so we can focus on rehearsal. :rocket:

Evan Zhao: I can MC and handle the calendar + MC script. Prefer pre-records for non-US speakers. Will collect 3-min intros and 6-min lightning demos.
Priya Natarajan: Ill own QA for partner demos. Plan: run each demo in staging with the actual dataset and capture a 5-min failover clip. If live demo breaks, we cut to the pre-record.
Diego Morales: Logistics note: I can pack & stage 30 demo kits at HQ. Need HS codes and invoice templates from legal for cross-border. Also confirm whether partners want co-branded packing slips.
Lia Gomez: Swag plan: 30 attendee bundles + 8 VIP speaker bundles (co-branded patches, USB-C cable, one-pager). Will create QR card linking to code repo + quick start. Anyone opposed to including a small promo sticker for partners?
Maria Chen: Sounds good. Decision: EU partners - ship direct (customs handled by partner). APAC & US - stage at Redwood. Lia, please flag any custom-label text for EU shipments.
Evan Zhao: Recording: we need attendee-facing stream + partner raw tracks. Ill take the stream. Priya, can you run a second capture of partner feed for post-edit?
Priya Natarajan: Yes. Ill record local capture (OBS) and upload to s3://redwood-events/devsummit/ after event. Reminder: scrub any PII from logs before upload.
Diego Morales: AV list: 1x HDMI encoder, 2x spare adapters (USB-C/miniDP), ethernet switch with POE, and power strips. Also request a static IP for demo rigs.
Maria Chen: Run-of-show draft below  please comment quickly.
Maria Chen: ```
08:30 Vendor load-in & kit check
09:15 Host intro + safety notices (3m)
09:20 Lightning demos (6 x 6m)
09:56 Short break + raffle (digital)
10:10 Partner hands-on stations (5 stations, 20m rotations)
11:30 Closing + survey link + post-event resources
```
Evan Zhao: For hands-on rotations lets limit partner-led live demos to 15m each with 5m buffer for swap. Otherwise AV handoff will be a mess.
Priya Natarajan: Adding checklist (quick):
``

preview:
Ethan Park: Quick kick-off note we need a condensed 2-day SI Accelerator for partners: day 1 marketplace & compliance; day 2 hardware compatibility & private deploys. Goal: partners leave with a working listing + a validated on-prem deploy. Dates?
Laila Singh: +1. Can we standardize a compatibility matrix for GPUs, PCIe gen, and driver stacks? That'll speed up validation.
Connor Briggs: I'll draft the matrix. Recommend we include Quantization guidance (int8 vs fp16) and perf baselines for 8k/32k contexts. Also add a troubleshooting appendix for OOMs.
Amelia Ruiz: For certification, propose tiers: Bronze = completed pre-work + MCQ; Silver = passed hands-on labs; Gold = completed a joint customer POC within 90 days. Badges live in the partner portal.
Ethan Park: Nice. Pre-work should be asynchronous (30-45 min) with a short checklist: account setup, sandbox keys, and a Console walkthrough video.
Laila Singh: Marketplace QA lab idea: partners create a sandbox listing, run an end-to-end purchase (test card), and validate telemetry ingestion into our Console. We'll need a mock metering payload.
Connor Briggs: I can create a small node image and an infra-as-code snippet for the lab:
```
- terraform apply marketplace-sandbox
- deploy sample service: ./deploy.sh --model small-open
- run billing smoke test: ./billing-test --org sandbox-123
```
Amelia Ruiz: Hardware vendor update: CoreCompute confirmed 2 loaner racks for the week prior to the bootcamp. They require an LOA and insurance certificate. I'll handle the paperwork.
Ethan Park: Ops ask: can Eng-Infra spin up isolated sandboxes per cohort? We should avoid cross-tenant state and ensure billing sandbox has non-production limits.
Laila Singh: Post-bootcamp follow-up: schedule 3 office-hour windows over two weeks and issue a lab artifact pack (VM snapshots, terraform state, log samples). Also add a troubleshooting ticket triage channel.
marketplace-bot: Event scheduled: "SI Accelerator: Marketplace + HW Labs" (May 23-24) — invites sent.
Connor Briggs: Final note on deliverables: 1) compatibility matrix + appendix, 2) instructor guide + grading rubric, 3) marketplace lab scripts + sandbox snapshot, 4) cert badge criteria doc. Who owns each?
Amelia Ruiz: I'll own vendor logistics & LOAs.
Ethan Park: I'll take instructor guide + rubric.
Laila Singh: I'll do matrix and lab scripts with Connor.
Connor Briggs: I'll prep the terraform/image artifacts and recording instructions.
Amelia Ruiz: Let's reconvene Fri 9am PT to lock the agenda and confirm the LOAs. :party_parrot:

title: #partnerships: 1818881234
preview:
Aisha Ramos: Quick sync — Fluxion wants to run a joint perf/W + throughput sweep next week. Need to lock lab window, telemetry contract, and a short media embargo plan. Who can own instrumentation?
Ben Carter: I can shepherd the kernel-tune side. Need access to their board console and our perf agents.
Mina Cho: I'll take telemetry ingestion and schema. We should standardize on these metrics: `throughput_tokens_s`, `p50_ms`, `p95_ms`, `joules_total`, `joules_per_token`. CSV + JSONL manifest.
Jeroen van Dijk: FYI their lab only allows signed IP ranges, so we'll need to add our test runner host IPs to allow outbound. Also ask for rack power sensor IDs.
Aisha Ramos: Good. Ben — can you post the minimal kernel patch list you need and a risk checklist? We prefer no vendor-blacked patches for embargo runs.
Ben Carter: Will do. Minimal list: `fix/sched-affinity-opts`, `nv-kernel-kv-cache-compat`, `mmap-pagepin-opt`. Risk checklist short: kernel rollback snapshot, golden-image checksum, watchdogs on.
Mina Cho: Telemetry plan: we'll ship a small agent that posts to a pre-signed S3 bucket and also streams summary to our ingestion endpoint `/ingest/partner-fluxion/v1`. Format example: 
```
{"run_id":"fluxion-2026-03-08-01","metrics":"csv","manifest":"s3://partner/fluxion/run_id/manifest.jsonl"}
```
Aisha Ramos: Legal/NDAs? Marketing?
Taylor (oss-marketing): Marketing wants a 48h quote review window before any embargo lift. Also request a single joint boilerplate quote from engineering by EOD Friday. We'll align on joint press timing (T+3 days after joint signoff).
Jeroen van Dijk: On data retention — partner requested 30 days. Our defaults are 90. Mina, can we support config to auto-delete after 30 days?
Mina Cho: Yes, we can set lifecycle rule on the S3 prefix for 30 days for partner runs. Will document retention in the run manifest.
Aisha Ramos: Great. Timeline suggestion:
- Mar 8 09:00 UTC: lab access window start
- Mar 8–9: runs + kernel tune iterations
- Mar 10: final verification + golden-run capture
- Mar 11: internal signoff + engineering quote to marketing
- Mar 14: embargo lift (if agreed)
Can everyone confirm they can make those windows?
Ben Carter: I can block Mar 8–10 on my calendar. Will prep rollback scripts by Mar 7.
Mina Cho: Available Mar 8 morning UTC for agent rollout. I'll post the ingestion test script later today: `scripts/telemetry-smoke.sh`.
Taylor (oss-marketing): I can do quote draft Mar 10 and sync with partner comms. Need approved technical blurb and one performance chart.
Aisha Ramos: Action items: Ben — kernel patch list + rollback docs; Mina — ingestion schema + smoke script + lifecycle rule; Taylor — embargo timeline + draft quote; Jeroen — IP whitelists + rack sensor mapping.
TelemetryBot: Ingestion endpoint `/ingest/partner-fluxion/v1` deployed to staging at 2026-03-04T14:12Z.
calendar-bot: Event created: "Fluxion joint bench — lab window" Mar 8 09:00 UTC — attendees: Aisha Ramos, Ben Carter, Mina Cho, Jeroen van Dijk, Taylor (oss-marketing).
Ben Carter: One more thing — please ensure we collect raw power traces at 1kHz for at least the golden runs. I'll include a tiny ffmpeg-like command for the sensor: `power-rec --device /dev/pwr0 --hz 1000 --out power.wav`.
Mina Cho: Noted. We'll accept `.wav` and `.csv` for power. Adding to manifest schema.
Aisha Ramos: Cool. I'll ping partner for IP ranges and the contact for on-site lab lead. If anything changes post-smoke, please update this thread only. :rocket: 
Taylor (oss-marketing): Also request a one-slide executive summary template — I'll send a quick draft. :eyes: 
Jeroen van Dijk: Sending rack sensor list now.
Mina Cho: Uploading ingestion smoke link: https://s3.console.aws.amazon.com/s3/object/redwood-partner/fluxion/smoke-2026-03-04.log
Aisha Ramos: Thanks team. We'll reconvene 24h before the run for a final go/no-go.
Ben Carter: Ack.
Taylor (oss-marketing): Ack — will have draft quote ready.
TelemetryBot: Reminder scheduled: "Telemetry smoke check" Mar 7 15:00 UTC.

title: #partnerships: 1795554321
preview:
Nora Vasquez: Heads-up: partner requested a lightweight Terraform "edge" kit for Marketplace that supports quick deploy on partner-managed clouds (AWS/GCP) and an optional GPU path. Core ask: minimal variables, clear SKU mapping, and CI that can produce signed artifacts.

Ethan Park: I like minimal. Proposed variables: cloud, region, env, enable_gpu=false, gpu_profile="". If enable_gpu=true and gpu_profile empty error out. Keep networking simple: use existing VPC if provided, else create a lean subnet.

Clara Liu: For the SKU mapping, I'll add a csv in /docs/sku_matrix.csv with columns: cloud, region, recommended_sku, notes. We'll also include a tiny helper script map_sku.py that reads desired_profile and prints recommended SKU.

Nora Vasquez: Packaging needs: tar.gz with manifest.json (semver, sha256, components list) and GPG signature. Partner legal wants the signature before publish.

publish-bot: Note: publish workflow requires the 'partner-cla' GitHub check.

Ethan Park: CI steps I propose: terraform fmt/validate, terraform plan (no apply, unless DRY_RUN=1), build artifact, compute sha256, create manifest.json, sign artifact (gpg --detach-sign), upload to s3://partner-artifacts/{partner}/{version}/, run smoke via remote sandbox if partner grants access.

DevRel Sam: Can we provide a tiny demo app (node/python) that calls Redwood Hosted API with env var for API key and prints a sample response? That makes the listing friendlier in marketplace UI.

Clara Liu: Yes — examples/simple-infer.py with retries + example.env. Also add a note about rate-limited demo keys and how to exchange for partner keys.

Nora Vasquez: Action items: Ethan (pipeline + terraform root) // Clara (docs + example app + SKU csv) // Sam (demo app sample content + marketplace listing blurb) // Nora (partner coordination & legal signoff). Meeting to review this Wednesday 11:00 PT.

Ethan Park: I'll prototype GH Action and share a link by Tue.

Clara Liu: I'll push the sku csv and map_sku.py to branch 'edge-lightkit' by EOD.

publish-bot: Reminder: partner-enablement review scheduled Wed 11:00 PT.

Nora Vasquez: Thanks team — let's keep the kit under 200 lines of Terraform and a 5-minute deploy demo. :rocket:
text:

Ethan Park: I like minimal. Proposed variables: cloud, region, env, enable_gpu=false, gpu_profile="". If enable_gpu=true and gpu_profile empty error out. Keep networking simple: use existing VPC if provided, else create a lean subnet.

Clara Liu: For the SKU mapping, I'll add a csv in /docs/sku_matrix.csv with columns: cloud, region, recommended_sku, notes. We'll also include a tiny helper script map_sku.py that reads desired_profile and prints recommended SKU.

Nora Vasquez: Packaging needs: tar.gz with manifest.json (semver, sha256, components list) and GPG signature. Partner legal wants the signature before publish.

Ethan Park: I'll prototype GH Action and share a link by Tue.

Nora Vasquez: Thanks team — let's keep the kit under 200 lines of Terraform and a 5-minute deploy demo. :rocket:

Samira Qureshi: Quick ask 1 1 - partner prepping marketplace listing + joint PR. Need to confirm what compliance artifacts we can surface publicly (SOC excerpt, pentest blurb, questionnaire summary). Can legal/sec sign off by Fri?

Noah Park: Upload the draft PR + proposed artifact list here. Do we have an NDA/embargo from partner? Don't claim control coverage unless it's verbatim from their attestation.

Ben Carter: Security: safe-public set = SOC attestation cover (redacted), one-paragraph pentest exec summary (no IPs/hosts/PoCs), sanitized integration diagram. Questionnaires should be summarized as "completed" not published verbatim.

Yara Morales: Legal prefers this SOC phrasing: "Partner holds SOC2 Type II attestation covering security principles; full reports available to partners under NDA." Also request an embargo clause for the announcement.

Samira Qureshi: Partner signed NDA in April. Timeline: want to announce Mon; partner expects 48h review after we share. That means we need final bundle Fri morning.

sec-bot: Reminder: redact hostnames/IPs, cert serials, internal urls. Replace any SLA/uptime claims with "subject to partner attestation and SLA."

Ben Carter: I'll draft the pentest blurb: 2-3 lines + CVSS counts and remediation status. Post redacted draft here for quick review Thu AM.

Noah Park: Presign plan: 7-day presigned S3 urls. Command we use: `aws s3 presign s3://redwood-internal/marketplace/partnerA/soc_cover_redacted.pdf --expires-in 604800`. Who will generate?

Yara Morales: Assign owners: SOC cover - Yara; Pentest summary - Ben; Diagram - Samira. I'll prepare a short legal checklist: attestation wording, embargo, no control IDs, NDA verified.

Samira Qureshi: I'll package artifacts and request presigns from infra. If legal/sec sign off by Fri 10am, we can share with partner and keep Monday target.

Noah Park: Noted. If partner asks for heavier redaction that breaks PR narrative, we'll propose alternative language. Ping me for escalation.

Ben Carter: Will post redacted pentest summary Thu 09:30 for review.

Samira Qureshi: Thanks all 1 :thumbsup:

title: #partnerships: 1789012345
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — CloudWave asked for a lightweight refarch + Terraform starter modules for an LLM Edge Accelerator proof-of-concept. Dropping the preliminary repo link and a short checklist. Who can own review + security review?

Diego Marquez: I can take module structure + CI smoke tests. Ping me the repo.

Ava Chen: Repo: https://github.com/redwood-inference/cloudwave-llm-edge-starter (private) — please don't share externally.

Priya Patel: I'll handle KMS/HSM integration notes and the audit logging runbook. ETA EOD Thu.

Samir Gupta: Are we supporting quantized variants in the starter kit or just float32 for now?

Ava Chen: Start with int8/int4 quant docs as opt-in. Add a sample `qc_profile` var in the module:

```hcl
variable "qc_profile" {
  type = string
  default = "none" # options: none, int8, int4
}
```

Priya Patel: Also add an example for KMS URI: `kms://projects/x/locations/y/keyRings/z/cryptoKeys/k`

Marketplace Bot: New ticket created: PART-4213 (CloudWave starter kit security checklist) — https://tracker.internal/PART-4213

Tina Wong: Important: we need a short "how to smoke test on tiny infra" runbook for the partner engineers. Minimal commands please.

Diego Marquez: I'll add a smoke test that spins a single node and runs `make smoke` — sample snippet:

```bash
# short smoke
terraform init
terraform apply -var='region=us-west1' -auto-approve
make smoke
```

Ava Chen: FYI product and sales want a joint blog/launch slot in 3 weeks — can we have a deliverable timeline?

Samir Gupta: Rough timeline: module + tests (1w), security review (1w), partner polish + docs (1w) — fits 3 weeks if no blockers.

Priya Patel: If HSM integration adds time, we'll flag it by Tue.

Tina Wong: I'll coordinate partner comms and schedule a technical review on Wed 10am PT. Calendar invite coming.

Ava Chen: Great — owners: Diego (module + CI), Priya (security/runbook), Tina (partner comms), Samir (runtime/quant guidance). I'll track in PART-4213. Thanks everyone :tada:

title: #partnerships: 1769998888
preview:
Priya Rao: heads up — EdgeConnect (SI) ran into a blocker during their private deployment for Acme Health. marketplace image passed basic checks but their on-prem nodes fail during node-driver init.
Ben Carter: can you paste the error/log snippet?
Priya Rao: sure — partner sent this from node0: `kernel: module 'accelerator_adapter' failed to load: version magic mismatch` and `redwood-agent: image attestation check failed: signer not trusted`.
Chris Liu: hmm looks like two separate issues: kernel module/driver ABI mismatch + image signing/trust chain. which one is blocking the go-live?
Priya Rao: both. driver prevents GPU binding, attestation blocks our marketplace install path (we enforce signed images for private deploys). EdgeConnect says their build system signs with an internal CI key not in our truststore.
Dana Ortiz: we need a short arch review with EdgeConnect + Eng Runtime + Partner SRE. short agenda: (1) confirm kernel/driver version target (2) signing key onboarding or alternate flow (3) fallback plan (use compatible driver from our repo or use dedicated nodes).
Ben Carter: available today 17:00 UTC or tomorrow 16:00 PDT. I can run the debugging session and share node access.
Chris Liu: Eng-runtime — can we surface a compatible driver shim we can push as a temporary fix? we have a signed, packaged `accelerator_adapter` v1.4 that works on RHEL 8.6 kernels.
eng-runtime: we can upstream v1.4 to the private image for the customer if they agree. need confirmation of kernel-ABI on their fleet.
Priya Rao: EdgeConnect said nodes are on RHEL 8.6 (kernel 4.18.0-425). I'll confirm exact `uname -r` tonight.
partner-sre: adding note — EdgeConnect CI key fingerprint is `AB:3F:9C:12:...` (they sent it). they asked for a short key onboarding process rather than re-signing all images.
Chris Liu: security-wise we prefer onboarding keys via marketplace attestation pipeline rather than ad-hoc trust additions. ops: how long to onboard a partner key?
infra-bot: ticket created: PARTNER-4821 — "EdgeConnect marketplace key onboarding + driver compatibility" assigned to eng-security, eng-runtime, partner-sre. ETA initial triage: 4h.
Ben Carter: okay we'll join the triage. For immediate go/no-go we should propose a temporary rollout path: 1) add our packaged v1.4 driver to customer's private image (signed by Redwood), 2) open key onboarding flow for EdgeConnect so future images pass attestation.
Dana Ortiz: add a fallback routing plan in case the private image approach fails: route inference traffic to Redwood Dedicated in the same region until on-prem fleet replaced. How long is their expected replacement window?
Priya Rao: EdgeConnect requested 2 weeks for full rebuild+CI change. they can accept a 3–5 day hotfix if we provide signed driver.
Chris Liu: action items: 1) Priya confirm uname and node metadata (tags, k8s version) — due 2h. 2) eng-runtime prepare signed image bump with v1.4 driver and smoke test — due EOD. 3) eng-security review onboarding checklist for partner key (PARTNER-4821) — due 24h. 4) Partner SRE coordinate cutover and run smoke with customer — scheduled after image ready.
Ben Carter: can someone drop the command we need to run on node to capture exact kernel+modules? I can run it when I get access.
Chris Liu: `uname -r && rpm -q kernel && lsmod | grep accelerator_adapter` and `journalctl -u redwood-agent -o short-iso --no-pager | tail -n 200`
partner-sre: confirmed meeting — 2025-04-03 16:00 PDT / 23:00 UTC. Calendar invite sent to EdgeConnect, Priya, Ben, Chris, Dana.
Priya Rao: invited eng-runtime and eng-security as optional. thanks.
Dana Ortiz: note for go-live checklist: update rollback doc with dedicated fallback, add telemetry flag so we can detect failed driver binds and auto-fallback after N=5 failures.
Ben Carter: will add a short eval prompt set to the smoke tests (latency/throughput) so we can quickly verify parity after hotfix.
Chris Liu: once eng-runtime posts the signed image digest please paste here — we'll verify attestation flow locally. also need to confirm our attestation service has contact with EdgeConnect CA or we instruct them to upload their public key to the partner key onboarding endpoint.
Priya Rao: copied. I'll get the digest and node info tonight. thanks everyone. :thumbsup:
Ben Carter: :pray:
partner-sre: keeping PARTNER-4821 updated — will log each milestone there.
eng-runtime: posted image digest in ticket: `sha256:0a1b2c3d4e5f...` (do not use in prod until security thumbs-up).
Priya Rao: received. I'll share with EdgeConnect for quick smoke tomorrow morning.
Dana Ortiz: moving to close this thread — we'll reconvene after initial smoke. if anything urgent ping #release-war-room.
Priya Rao: closing loop here. thanks all!"
