title: #partnerships: 1769990011
preview:
Lina Park: quick sync please 1 - final readiness for NimbusEdge + SI Pack marketplace launch. primary asks: sandbox for SI validation, press embargo rules, hero assets, SKU mapping, and day-0 support rota. target Wed 07:00 PT.
Jonah Reed: creative still needs badge SVG + 2 hero crops. asked Nimbus for source files. if late we fallback to neutral hero and hide partner badge.
Priya Shah: legal: we can do one early hands-on under NDA. will draft a single-use NDA addendum. need outlet name and tester emails.
Mateo Alvarez: ops: sandbox can be provisioned but need exact spec. proposed: 2x A10G-ish nodes, 60GB nvme, 8 vCPU, locked egress. is that ok?
Zoe Chen: marketing: embargo plan 1 - prefer Tue 10am PT embargo to bloggers, Wed 07:00 PT public. if outlet requests early hands-on we require NDA signed before access.
Samir Patel: SI lead here 1 - we need a 1-pager quickstart + a tiny runbook (3 bullets) and a contact email. I can draft quickstart tonight.
Lina Park: perfect. Samir also please include a 2-line troubleshooting FAQ for landing page.
Samir Patel: will do. also request a tiny "1-click deploy" snippet for the docs so SI can validate quickly.
Jonah Reed: creative asks for a 1s install GIF for the microsite hero slot. Nimbus said they can supply an MP4 and a static PNG fallback.
Priya Shah: NDA addendum will be ready in ~2h. who will collect signatures?
Lina Park: partnerships will coordinate signatures with Nimbus AM. Priya please drop the addendum link here when ready.
Mateo Alvarez: ops question: any objections to a temporary routing rule for first 48h to route inference-heavy requests to Dedicated pool (and fallback to quantized variant if latency > 350ms)? reduces cost blast risk.
Zoe Chen: agree. product + eng-platform should sign off but thumbs up from marketing.
Samir Patel: can we also surface a short note on the marketplace listing: "SI integration support hours: Wed-Fri 9-5 PT" and include the triage email? reduces confusion.
Lina Park: yes add that to the listing. Jonah can you reserve a CTA slot for "SI office hours"?
Jonah Reed: reserved. landing will have CTA + mini checklist: Install, Auth, Smoke-test.
eng-bot: doc created: https://redwood.docs/marketplace/nimbusedge-si-quickstart v0.9 (auto-tagged)
Priya Shah: NDA addendum uploaded: https://redwood.legal/nimbus-handson-addendum v1.0
Lina Park: timeline draft:```
- Mon AM: final images + SKU list in repo
- Mon noon: NDA signed for early hands-on (if used)
- Mon EOD: one-off early hands-on (NDA'd) if approved
- Tue AM: sandbox provisioned + SI smoke tests start
- Tue 17:00 PT: landing page staging cutover
- Wed 07:00 PT: blog + public announcement
- Wed 09:00 PT: marketplace listing live
- Thu 11:00 PT: first analytics + cost spike review
```
Mateo Alvarez: ops note: please confirm SKU mapping name convention so we don't mismatch marketplace IDs. send CSV with fields: sku_id, model_variant, region.
Samir Patel: SI will validate SKUs during sandbox smoke tests. will post results in this thread.
Zoe Chen: PR: if an outlet asks for an embargo exception, PR wants full outlet name + justification. we only allow one NDA'd early hands-on for a single outlet.
Lina Park: Nimbus suggested "InfraToday" as the outlet for early hands-on. PR, proceed with NDA flow?
Zoe Chen: proceed. PR will handle outreach once NDA signed.
Jonah Reed: creative fallback: if Nimbus images miss the cutover we will use a neutral hero and add a subtle "partner badge coming" pill. prevents blocking listing.
Priya Shah: legal confirms addendum allows single-use access and prohibits screenshots until public release. good.
eng-bot: runbook updated: /playbooks/marketplace-nimbusedge-golive v3.0 (fallback routing added)
Mateo Alvarez: provisioning note: sandbox creds expire 48h after creation. who should own rotation and invites?
Samir Patel: SI lead will own invites and initial rotation. ops keep rotation automated after smoke tests.
Lina Park: final blocker check 1 - anything else?
Jonah Reed: ask creative: do we support localized landing (JP/FR) at launch or later? Nimbus requested JP.
Zoe Chen: recommend EN-only at launch and wave 2 localization week 2. communicate expectations to Nimbus.
Lina Park: agreed. I'll notify Nimbus AM and ask for final assets by Mon EOD.
calendar-bot: event created: NimbusEdge Marketplace Launch 1 Wed 07:00 PT (invitees: partnerships, PR, marketing, eng-platform)
Lina Park: thanks team. I'll post a single checklist link here after lunch and update playbook with the sandbox spec.

Mateo Alvarez: provisioning note: sandbox creds expire 48h after creation. who should own rotation and invites?
Samir Patel: SI lead will own invites and initial rotation. ops keep rotation automated after smoke tests.
Lina Park: final blocker check 1 - anything else?
Jonah Reed: ask creative: do we support localized landing (JP/FR) at launch or later? Nimbus requested JP.

Lina Park: agreed. I'll notify Nimbus AM and ask for final assets by Mon EOD.

title: #partnerships: 1769584432
preview:
Lukas Meyer: GCP partner team wants a concrete testing plan before they’ll move our submission forward. Can we align on an E2E plan for GCP marketplace: subscription → entitlement → Redwood org provisioning (staging + prod) + how we prove metering is correct?

Lukas Meyer: Drafting something like below, please poke holes / add steps:
1) In GCP marketplace, subscribe to Redwood listing (test buyer project)
2) GCP creates entitlement
3) Redwood entitlement sync job picks it up
4) User lands in console marketplace entrypoint, links/creates org
5) Entitlement verified (backend) and limits applied
6) First API key → first successful request
7) Metering dims show up in GCP billing export / internal billing reconciliation

Chris Osei: +1. Let’s make it explicit for staging vs prod since GCP asked “what exactly are you testing in production.” Also we should include failure-mode tests (entitlement delay, wrong project, resubscribe, etc.).

Leila Farouk: I can own a QA checklist + a repeatable runbook. Questions:
- Do we have a GCP “test buyer” billing account/project already, or do we need Partnerships to request one?
- For staging: do we have a separate GCP listing/test environment or are we testing against prod listing but pointing to staging callback/config?

Ethan Park: For staging we can validate most of the flow without touching the public listing by using our staging procurement config + a partner-provided test entitlement (GCP can generate entitlements for test). For prod we’ll do a minimal live purchase with a Redwood-owned GCP project/billing account.

Cole Summers: Metering verification needs to be part of this plan. Specifically:
- Subscription SKU → internal meter mapping (Hosted tokens, embeddings, Dedicated capacity, add-ons)
- Usage posted under correct GCP billing dimensions
- Edge case: when entitlement is present but org not linked yet, we should not start metering

Chloe Martin: Console-wise, test plan should include:
- “Continue from GCP Marketplace” entrypoint
- Happy path: new org vs existing org
- Error states:
  a) entitlement not found yet (propagation delay)
  b) entitlement belongs to different GCP project
  c) entitlement already linked to a different Redwood org
  d) user lacks permissions to create API keys
- Retry guidance copy is important (we got feedback on this last review).

Nadia Rahman: Telemetry checks:
- event: `mp_gcp_entitlement_lookup_started/finished` (latency)
- event: `mp_gcp_org_link_succeeded/failed` (reason)
- event: `mp_gcp_first_api_key_created`
- event: `mp_gcp_first_successful_request`
We should call out expected dashboards + what “good” looks like (p50/p95 entitlement lookup, failure rate thresholds).

Ben Carter: For prod test, can we add “time-to-first-token” measurement? GCP team asked about buyer experience and we should have a number for: subscription completed → first 200 OK request. Also note any human steps (e.g., waiting for entitlement propagation).

Lukas Meyer: OK, I’ll turn this into a concrete checklist. Proposed structure:

STAGING (pre-submission / regression)
Pre-reqs:
- Redwood staging env configured with GCP marketplace integration creds
- GCP partner team provides test entitlement OR we have access to their sandbox flow
- Redwood-owned test user accounts: 1 admin, 1 non-admin

Test cases:
S1. New customer, new org
- Subscribe (or inject test entitlement)
- Verify entitlement appears in Redwood (entitlement sync job log)
- In console: create new org via marketplace entrypoint
- Create first API key
- Make first request (chat/completions) and confirm response + usage shows in console

S2. Existing Redwood org (already has usage-based billing)
- Link marketplace entitlement to existing org
- Confirm billing source switches to “Marketplace (GCP)” and previous billing remains intact (no double-charge)

S3. Entitlement propagation delay
- Attempt to link immediately after subscribe
- Confirm error state shown + “try again” works once entitlement arrives
- Confirm we do not provision org/limits prematurely

S4. Wrong GCP project
- Attempt link using a different GCP project than the entitlement
- Confirm we get deterministic error + no org changes

S5. Unsubscribe / entitlement suspended
- Simulate entitlement state change
- Confirm Redwood access downgraded per policy (grace period?) and requests blocked appropriately

S6. Multiple entitlements
- If buyer subscribes to Hosted + Compliance add-on (or multiple SKUs), confirm we attach both to the same org and enforce correct feature flags/limits

PRODUCTION (post-submission smoke)
Pre-reqs:
- Redwood-owned GCP billing account + project (finance-approved)
- Final listing/offer is live

Test cases:
P1. One real subscription purchase (smallest paid SKU)
- Run full happy path: subscribe → link org → API key → first request
- Capture timestamps for: subscribe complete, entitlement visible, org linked, first 200 OK

P2. Metering validation
- Generate controlled usage (e.g., 50k tokens + 100 embeddings calls)
- Confirm:
  - internal usage logs show correct marketplace dimension ids
  - GCP billing export shows the same dimensions (with expected delay)
  - reconciliation matches within tolerance

P3. Cancellation
- Cancel subscription
- Confirm access changes according to entitlement state; confirm no further metering events emitted after suspension

Evidence artifacts to share with GCP reviewer:
- Screenshots: entitlement detected in console, org linked, API key created
- Log snippets (redacted): entitlement sync success, verify endpoint returning OK
- Metering proof: internal usage record id ↔ GCP dimension name (with delay noted)

Owners:
- QA/runbook: Leila
- Control plane / entitlement sync logs: Ethan
- Meter mapping + reconciliation: Cole + Nadia
- Console UX validation: Chloe
- Tracking + partner comms: Lukas + Chris

Leila Farouk: Looks good. One addition: add explicit “permissions” test (non-admin user tries to link org / create key and gets blocked). That’s a common enterprise snag.

Naomi Feldman: Please also add a security sanity check line item:
- confirm audit log entries are generated for marketplace org link + API key creation
- confirm we’re not storing any unnecessary GCP buyer metadata beyond entitlement identifiers
No need for a deep dive, but GCP sometimes asks.

Ethan Park: +1 on audit logs. We can pull the audit events for:
- `marketplace.entitlement.linked`
- `apikey.created`
- `org.created`
and attach screenshots for the reviewer.

Cole Summers: For metering: call out expected delays. GCP billing export is not real-time; we should state “validate within 24h” (or whatever Finance is comfortable with). Also: make sure we test dimension names exactly as they appear in GCP portal.

Nadia Rahman: I’ll add the dashboard links + thresholds once we agree, but for the plan:
- entitlement lookup p95 < 2s (excluding propagation)
- org link failure rate < 2%
- first request completion rate > 90% within 30 min of subscription (baseline improvement target)

Chris Osei: Perfect. Lukas, once you write this up, drop it in the partner review tracker + we’ll send to GCP with the “what we can test before listing is live” vs “what we will smoke test after launch.”

Lukas Meyer: Will do. I’ll post a v1 doc in an hour and @mention everyone for final pass. Also: I’ll ask Finance for approval on the prod subscription/billing account so we’re not blocked later.

title: #partnerships: 1769527811
preview:
Stephanie Nguyen: Need approvals today/tomorrow on the *standard AWS Private Offer template* + discount guardrails so we can stop reinventing the wheel per deal. Draft doc here: https://drive.google.com/redwood/aws-private-offer-template-v1 (comment mode on). @Jordan Blake @Laura Bennett @Michael Grant @Sofia Mendes can you review + sign off? Also need an explicit “when to escalate” rule for anything outside guardrails.

Chris Osei: +1. This is blocking the marketplace refresh timeline (AWS submissions + enablement). Would love a clear RACI + which fields are “fixed” vs “editable per deal.”

Jordan Blake: Skimmed. Directionally good. My main concern is discount creep + reps promising custom terms. Can we lock in hard guardrails and a clean escalation path? I want this usable by AEs without a 3-day approval chain.

Laura Bennett: Finance view: we can support a standard template if we’re explicit about (1) min annual commit, (2) max discount by segment, (3) payment schedule, and (4) usage true-ups vs prepaid. Right now the doc says “discount up to 25%” with no segmenting.

Sofia Mendes: Legal view: template is fine but we need to be careful with the language “SLA-backed” in the offer doc unless the customer is buying Dedicated/Private with the SLA addendum attached. Also: AWS Private Offers sometimes require mirroring the Marketplace EULA + our Order Form. Let’s ensure the template references the right Redwood terms package.

Michael Grant: Adding: we should not promise data residency or “no training on customer data” beyond what’s in our published security packet / DPA. I see a section “Data handling notes” in the draft—needs standard wording + link to the security docs, not custom claims.

Stephanie Nguyen: Got it. Proposal for guardrails section (open to edits):
- Standard term: 12 months (default), optional 24 months with Finance approval
- Standard payment: annual upfront; net-30 only (no net-60)
- Max discount (list): up to 10% (Self-serve/Hosted heavy), up to 15% (mid-market), up to 20% (enterprise) *only with CRO approval*
- Min annual commit: $60k Hosted, $120k Dedicated, Private is “quote only”
- No custom SLA language in private offer doc; refer to standard SLA addendum if applicable
Would this satisfy Finance/Legal?

Laura Bennett: Close. I’d rather we do:
- Max discount without CRO: 10% (all segments)
- 15% requires CRO + Finance
- 20% requires CRO + Finance + RevRec review (bc it often implies weird structuring)
Also minimums: for Dedicated we should tie to a capacity reservation floor, not just $ amount. But as a guardrail, $120k is OK as long as we call it “minimum annual commitment equivalent.”

Emily Stone: RevRec: please avoid language implying “prepaid credits” unless we’re treating it as usage-based with variable consideration. For private offers we should keep it simple:
- either contract-based fixed fee (Dedicated reservation)
- or usage-based billed through marketplace with a committed spend floor
If template says “credits roll over” etc, it complicates revrec.

Jordan Blake: Agree w/ Laura. Also I want a single sentence in the guardrails: “Any deviation from standard terms requires written approval in #finance + #legal + #partnerships.” Otherwise we’ll get side DMs.

Sofia Mendes: +1. Specific legal edits:
1) Replace “Redwood will provide 99.9% uptime” with “per Redwood SLA” and only if SLA addendum is attached.
2) Remove “Most favored pricing” language entirely (I saw it in an optional clause section).
3) Add: “This Private Offer incorporates the Marketplace terms + Redwood Order Form v2026-01” (or whatever the correct version is).
4) Add a disclaimer that support SLA depends on support tier / SKU.

Michael Grant: I left comments in the doc around the “Security & compliance” section. Suggest we use a safe block:
“Security posture and controls are described in the Redwood Marketplace Security Overview (link). Any additional requirements are addressed via DPA / security addendum.”
Also, remove “HIPAA ready” from any template language.

Isabelle Laurent: Ops question: where does this live + what’s the process for AEs? Ideally: one canonical doc + a checklist + required HubSpot fields. Otherwise we’ll still have inconsistent submissions.

Chris Osei: We can link it from the internal enablement page and add a HubSpot workflow note (Isabelle can own that). For this thread: can we get “approved with changes” today and move the doc to final by EOD tomorrow?

Markus Klein: From SE side: please keep one editable section for “Deployment scope / region / SKU” because customers ask. But agree we shouldn’t add custom security claims. Also add a standard line about entitlement timing (AWS sometimes takes ~15–60 min). Saves us support escalations.

Stephanie Nguyen: Summarizing proposed *guardrails v1* (based on comments):
- Default term: 12 months. 24 months requires Finance approval.
- Payment: annual upfront default; net-30 only. Anything else = Finance + Legal.
- Discounting:
  - <=10%: AE can proceed (no CRO) if minimums met
  - 10–15%: CRO + Finance approval
  - 15–20%: CRO + Finance + RevRec approval
  - >20%: not allowed (must be exception with exec sign-off)
- Minimum annual commitment equivalent:
  - Hosted: $60k
  - Dedicated: $120k (or capacity-reservation floor; follow-up in v1.1)
  - Private: quote only (no public guardrail)
- No custom SLA text; reference standard SLA addendum only when applicable.
- Security language: standard block + link to published security overview; no “HIPAA ready,” no bespoke residency promises.
- Any deviation requires written approval in channel (thread) with tagged approvers.
If everyone’s good, I’ll update the doc and mark as “v1 approved” once we have explicit yeses.

Jordan Blake: Approved pending those edits. :thumbsup: Please add one more bullet: “No free months” (discount only via %). I’ve seen reps try to do 14 months for 12.

Laura Bennett: Approved with Jordan’s “no free months” addition. Also please add “No retroactive credits/refunds via marketplace” unless Finance signs off.

Emily Stone: Approved w/ the revrec-safe wording and no “rollover credits.” If you keep it as “committed spend floor + usage billed through marketplace,” that’s usually fine.

Sofia Mendes: Approved once the SLA + security wording is corrected and the correct terms package is referenced (Order Form version + Marketplace EULA). I’ll review the final text after you incorporate comments.

Michael Grant: Same—approved pending edits. I’ll do a quick legal read once you update.

Isabelle Laurent: Approved from ops side. Once final, I’ll add a HubSpot snippet + checklist link in the deal workflow doc.

Stephanie Nguyen: Great. I’ll update doc now, resolve comments, and post back the final link + “copy/paste block” for AEs. Targeting EOD today for final review, tomorrow morning for enablement rollout.

Chris Osei: Thank you. When final is ready, I’ll link it in the marketplace enablement bundle + ping #sales and #partnerships with the guardrails summary.

Stephanie Nguyen: Updated doc: https://drive.google.com/redwood/aws-private-offer-template-v1-final (renamed “v1-final”). Incorporated:
- No free months
- Discount approval tiers
- Removed MFN clause
- SLA wording now “per Redwood SLA addendum if attached”
- Security block links to marketplace security overview
- Payment + refunds language clarified
@Sofia Mendes @Michael Grant can you confirm final legal wording is OK to roll out?

Sofia Mendes: Reviewed. Looks good. One nit: change “Order Form v2026-01” to “Order Form v2026-01-15” (latest). Otherwise approved.

Michael Grant: Confirmed. Approved. :white_check_mark:

Stephanie Nguyen: Done (updated version string). Calling this approved + rolling out. Thanks all.

- SLA wording now “per Redwood SLA addendum if attached”
- Security block links to marketplace security overview
- Payment + refunds language clarified
@Sofia Mendes @Michael Grant can you confirm final legal wording is OK to roll out?

Sofia Mendes: Reviewed. Looks good. One nit: change “Order Form v2026-01” to “Order Form v2026-01-15” (latest). Otherwise approved.

Michael Grant: Confirmed. Approved. :white_check_mark:

Stephanie Nguyen: Done (updated version string). Calling this approved + rolling out. Thanks all.

title: #partnerships: 1769453320
preview:
Lukas Meyer: Heads up — got the latest note from our GCP Marketplace reviewer. They’re not blocking yet, but they *will* if we don’t attach the required artifacts in the Producer Portal submission. Dropping the list here + proposed deadlines so we can assign owners.

Lukas Meyer: **GCP listing required artifacts (SaaS) — v1 checklist**
1) **Security / privacy docs (buyer-facing):**
- Security overview (shared responsibility, data handling, encryption in transit/at rest, KMS) 
- Data retention + deletion policy (defaults + configurable options)
- Audit logging overview (what events, export options)
- Compliance posture statement (SOC 2 / ISO wording ok, but no over-claims)
2) **Architecture diagrams:**
- High-level system architecture diagram (where Redwood runs vs customer)
- Data flow diagram (what data is processed/stored; where logs go)
3) **Support + SLA info:**
- Support contact (email + support portal URL)
- Support hours + target response times by severity (they want this explicitly)
- Uptime/SLA statement (even if “commercially reasonable” for Hosted; explicit SLA only for Dedicated/Private)
4) **Marketplace “How it works” / onboarding:**
- Step-by-step subscription → entitlement → org provisioning → first API key → first request
- Troubleshooting section for entitlement propagation delays
5) **Commercial / legal attachments:**
- Standard terms/EULA link (or PDF)
- Privacy policy link
- Any “usage metering / billing dimensions” description (mapping must be consistent with the technical integration)
6) **Listing assets:**
- Logo (hi-res, square)
- 3–6 screenshots (Console, usage dashboard, model catalog, audit log view if available)
- Optional: 60–90s explainer video (nice-to-have)

Lukas Meyer: **Timeline from GCP:**
- If we submit a complete package by *Feb 3 EOD PT*, they estimate first review feedback within ~5 business days.
- Missing security/support artifacts typically adds a full extra review cycle.

Lukas Meyer: Can folks sanity-check the above + call out anything I missed? Also need owners for each bucket.

Chris Osei: This matches what I heard on the last partner sync. The reviewer specifically called out “support SLAs” and “data retention” as common misses. +1 on Feb 3 as internal content freeze if we want a Feb 10–12 launch window.

Soojin Lee: Re: **billing dimensions** — GCP wants the public listing to describe *what* is metered (tokens, embeddings, etc.) without getting too into our internal meter IDs. But the “pricing plan” config in Producer Portal needs dimensions that match the integration exactly. I’ll pull from the SKU catalog v2 and draft a short buyer-facing blurb.

Camila Reyes: For **screenshots**, can we confirm minimum count + preferred aspect? Last time we got dinged because we used a wide banner crop.

Lukas Meyer: Camila — reviewer note says min 3 screenshots, max 15. Recommended 16:9 or 4:3, but avoid super-wide banners. They want readable UI text (no tiny type). Also: no competitor callouts in images.

Tessa Morgan: I can own **architecture diagram + data flow diagram**. Need confirmation on what we’re comfortable showing publicly (esp. the control plane pieces). If we’re aligning to the marketplace security packet, I’ll mirror that style.

Naomi Feldman: On the **security docs**, we should keep it “marketplace-friendly”: 
- State SOC 2 Type II is available *under NDA / via Trust Center* (don’t attach the full report to the listing)
- Provide a short control summary + scope statement
- Be precise on retention defaults + configurable retention (don’t imply “no logs”)
If we attach PDFs, please route through Security for wording approval.

Olga Petrov: +1. I can provide approved **compliance posture language** (SOC 2 / ISO-aligned) and the “how to request evidence” paragraph. We should not upload raw audit evidence into the portal.

Marcus Lin: For **audit logging** doc: we should include at least:
- auth events, API key creation/rotation, org linking, model changes, admin actions
- export options (SIEM) + retention for audit logs themselves
I’ll review Elliot/Alex’s draft for accuracy.

Alex Martinez: I can take first pass at **How it works / onboarding** doc text. We already have the flow in the design spec; I’ll adapt it to GCP language (subscription/entitlement in GCP terms).

Elliot Price: +1, and I’ll own **final docs URLs + IA** so we don’t have link churn during review. GCP reviewer asked that links be stable and publicly accessible (no “employees only” pages). We should decide now what’s public vs “on request.”

Ben Carter: For troubleshooting, can we explicitly mention the common case: “entitlement may take up to X minutes to propagate” + “retry entitlement check” + where to contact support? This is the biggest drop-off today.

Stephanie Nguyen: On **support SLAs** — we should be careful not to accidentally promise enterprise SLA on the Hosted SKU. Suggest we publish:
- Hosted: standard support targets (business hours + sev-based response)
- Dedicated/Private: “SLA-backed support per order form / private offer”
I can draft the severity table and send to Naomi for review.

Camila Reyes: Asset owners proposal:
- Screenshots: Alicia + me (I’ll coordinate capture list)
- Logo: Marketing ops already has the marketplace pack
We’ll need a final “approved set” by end of next week if we want time for review.

Chris Osei: Assignments (proposed) — reply with objections:
- Security overview + retention + audit logs buyer-facing pack: @Naomi Feldman (approval), @Olga Petrov (compliance language), @Marcus Lin (tech review)
- Diagrams: @Tessa Morgan
- Support SLA table + contacts: @Stephanie Nguyen
- Onboarding/how-it-works + troubleshooting: @Alex Martinez + @Ben Carter
- Final docs URLs + portal link validation: @Elliot Price
- Listing assets (logo/screenshots): @Camila Reyes
- Billing dimensions blurb: @Soojin Lee

Lukas Meyer: Works for me. One more callout from reviewer: they want the **support contact** to be explicit (support@… is fine) and not “contact sales.” Also want a URL that doesn’t require login.

Elliot Price: Noted. We can use docs.redwoodinference.com/support + support@redwoodinference.com (assuming that alias is ok). If we don’t have the public support page yet, we should create a lightweight one.

Naomi Feldman: Please don’t publish any statements like “HIPAA compliant” or “PCI compliant” in the listing artifacts unless Legal signs off. Stick to “supports regulated workloads” + list controls (encryption, audit logs, private deployment).

Soojin Lee: I’ll keep the billing blurb product-neutral: “metered by tokens processed (input/output) and embeddings” + “Dedicated billed as reserved capacity where applicable” and we’ll align to the actual GCP dimensions in portal config.

Lukas Meyer: Great. Final question: do we have a *hard* internal deadline for artifacts to Partnerships so we can package the submission?

Chris Osei: Suggest **Feb 2 12pm PT** for final artifacts handed to Partnerships (gives us buffer for a last pass + PDF conversions). Feb 3 EOD PT is submission.

Camila Reyes: Feb 2 noon PT is tight but doable for screenshots if we lock the capture list tomorrow.

Tessa Morgan: Diagrams by Feb 1 EOD PT. I’ll share drafts earlier for Naomi/MARCUS review.

Marcus Lin: I can review security/audit docs within 24h of draft.

Lukas Meyer: Cool — I’ll update the partner review tracker with this checklist + deadlines and ping the channel if GCP adds anything else.

title: #partnerships: 1769301123
preview:
Stephanie Nguyen: Kicking off SKU naming thread for marketplace refresh. Need one convention that works in AWS + GCP portals + matches internal billing catalog. Can we lock this this week?

Soojin Lee: +1. PMM needs stable names for listings/FAQs. Also avoid anything that partner reviewers flag as misleading ("unlimited", "guaranteed", etc.).

Cole Summers: From billing side: we need names to map cleanly to meter IDs. Ideally the human-facing SKU name and the internal SKU slug share a common base so support/finance can reconcile invoices.

Chris Osei: Constraints recap from last sync:
- AWS: product/offer "dimensions" have short identifiers + a public display name. Identifiers can’t be edited after publish (or it’s painful).
- GCP: billing dimensions are super picky about uniqueness; display name is what buyers see in console.
- Internally: billing catalog expects `plan_family` (hosted/dedicated/private) + `commitment` + `add_on`.
Let’s propose a naming pattern + examples.

Lukas Meyer: EMEA note: procurement folks prefer very plain names, and they compare line items. If we name something “Enterprise Ultra” it’s a red flag. Also need residency/compliance add-on to be obvious.

Laura Bennett: Finance ask: keep "Commit" and time period explicit when it’s a contract dimension. Also please don’t put $ amounts in SKU names.

Sofia Mendes: Legal: avoid “SOC2 compliant” in SKU title. Can say “Compliance add-on” but not imply certification is a product feature. Also avoid cloud provider trademarks in the SKU (e.g., "AWS Hosted") if we can.

Ben Carter: Growth/onboarding: names must be understandable at entitlement/provisioning step. Users see the SKU name when they link subscription to Redwood org. If it’s cryptic, support volume goes up.

Soojin Lee: Proposal v1 (pattern):
**Redwood Inference — {Deployment} — {Commercial model}**
Deployment: Hosted API | Dedicated Capacity | Private Deployment
Commercial model:
- Usage (PAYG)
- Commit (Annual) / Commit (Monthly) if marketplace supports
Add-ons: Compliance Pack | Premium Support | Extended Retention (if we ship)

Cole Summers: I like it, but we need shorter strings for AWS dimension identifiers. Suggest:
- Public display name: long, human-friendly
- Dimension code (AWS/GCP identifier): short + stable, like `hosted_payg_tokens`, `dedicated_commit_gpuhr`, etc.
We should not overload “SKU name” to mean both.

Stephanie Nguyen: Right, but the ask from partnerships is “SKU naming conventions across AWS/GCP”. Let’s define:
1) Listing product name (what shows on AWS/GCP listing)
2) Plan/SKU display names (what appears on the subscribe/charges page)
3) Dimension identifiers (codes)

Chris Osei: +1. Also: AWS team said long names get truncated in some views (esp. buyer’s “Subscriptions” page). Keep display names ~50 chars if possible.

Soojin Lee: Updated draft:
Product (listing): **Redwood Inference (LLM Inference Platform)**
Plans (display):
- **Hosted API (Usage-based)**
- **Dedicated Capacity (Contract)**
- **Private Deployment (Contract)**
Add-ons (display):
- **Compliance Pack**
- **Premium Support**

Lukas Meyer: Dedicated vs Private need to be crystal. Dedicated = Redwood-managed dedicated GPU pools; Private = VPC/on-prem options. Can we add “VPC/On‑prem” in the Private plan name?

Sofia Mendes: Careful with “On‑prem” if the marketplace SKU is only VPC and on-prem is a separate process/contract. If we can deliver on-prem under that SKU, fine; if not, keep it as “Private (VPC)”.

Naomi Feldman: Security POV: if we call it “Private Deployment (VPC)” buyers will expect customer-managed networking and isolation. That matches our posture. Also for Compliance Pack, we should align wording with the security packet: “Compliance Documentation & Controls” maybe.

Laura Bennett: Also want to avoid "Contract" for things that are actually metered overage. For Dedicated we might have base commit + overage. Naming should not imply hard cap.

Cole Summers: Metering mapping:
- Hosted API is token-based usage. Internally we have meters for input/output tokens and embeddings.
- Dedicated is capacity reservation (GPU-hours) + optional token overage.
- Private is platform fee + support tier (not token metering in the same way).
If we keep plan display names generic and let dimensions describe the billing, we’re good.

Stephanie Nguyen: AWS portal constraint: For SaaS Contract + metering, the "dimension names" are visible to buyers in the billing breakdown. If we put “hosted_payg_tokens” as the dimension name, that’s ugly.

Cole Summers: Yep. Suggest:
- Dimension *codes* short + stable
- Dimension *display names* buyer-friendly (AWS allows both; GCP has similar concept via SKU + metric label)
Example:
Code: `tokens_in_k` / Display: “Input tokens (per 1K)”
Code: `tokens_out_k` / Display: “Output tokens (per 1K)”
Code: `embeddings_k` / Display: “Embeddings (per 1K vectors)”

Soojin Lee: For GCP, we should avoid “per 1K vectors” if the official unit is per 1K requests or per 1K tokens. Need to match what our billing pipeline actually reports.

Cole Summers: Internally embeddings are billed per 1K input tokens equivalent today for most models. But marketplace wants a simple unit. If we label it “Embeddings (per 1K requests)” we have to meter requests, not tokens.

Chris Osei: Let’s not solve units here; thread is naming. But we should pick names that don’t lock us into the wrong unit.

Ben Carter: +1. Buyers mostly care that it’s understandable. "Embeddings units" is vague but safe.

Stephanie Nguyen: OK so for naming: keep dimensions generic enough to not misrepresent the unit.
Proposed dimension display names:
- “Text generation input”
- “Text generation output”
- “Embeddings”
- “Rerank requests”

Cole Summers: Works, and we can put exact units in the listing FAQ / pricing section.

Lukas Meyer: Back to plan names: can we standardize across AWS/GCP:
Hosted = “Hosted API (Usage-based)”
Dedicated = “Dedicated Capacity (Reserved)”
Private = “Private Deployment (VPC)”
Add-on = “Compliance Pack”

Soojin Lee: I’m ok with those. “Reserved” reads better than “Contract”. “Usage-based” is clear.

Laura Bennett: “Reserved” also maps to finance language (reserved capacity). Fine.

Sofia Mendes: “Compliance Pack” is ok if we’re careful in description: documentation, controls, audit log retention options, etc. But don’t imply certification.

Naomi Feldman: Suggest “Compliance Pack (Docs + Controls)” in the description, not the name.

Isabelle Laurent: CRM/ops note: whatever we choose should match HubSpot picklists (marketplace_sku). Please don’t rename after we roll out, it breaks reporting.

Chris Osei: Summarizing convention v2:
Listing product name: “Redwood Inference (LLM Inference Platform)”
Plan display names (AWS/GCP):
1) Hosted API (Usage-based)
2) Dedicated Capacity (Reserved)
3) Private Deployment (VPC)
Add-on display names:
- Compliance Pack
- Premium Support
Dimension display names: human-friendly service labels (no internal codes)
Dimension codes: stable snake_case matching internal meter family

Stephanie Nguyen: Any objections to “Private Deployment (VPC)” vs “Private (VPC)”?

Lukas Meyer: Prefer the longer one. Buyers understand “Private Deployment” better.

Soojin Lee: Agree.

Cole Summers: Fine for display. For internal slug we’ll use `private_vpc`.

Ben Carter: Can we ensure the console shows the display name + a short explanation (“You subscribed to: Private Deployment (VPC)”)? That reduces “what did I buy” tickets.

Chris Osei: Noted; that’s in onboarding UX workstream but we’ll feed this naming into Chloe’s team.

Stephanie Nguyen: Action items:
- Soojin: update copy guidelines to use these exact plan names.
- Cole: propose final dimension codes + display names list that won’t break billing catalog.
- Sofia/Naomi: sanity check “Compliance Pack” description language.
- Isabelle: align HubSpot picklists.
We’ll consider this “tentative approved” pending partner portal validation.

Cole Summers: I’ll post a mapping table here once I’ve checked against `billing_catalog.yaml` constraints (length, allowed chars).

Soojin Lee: :thumbsup:

Chris Osei: Dropping one more constraint from AWS: dimension *codes* can’t start with a number and should be <= 32 chars. Also avoid hyphens.

Cole Summers: good call.

(Next day)

Cole Summers: Draft dimension codes (stable) + display labels (buyer-facing):
- Code: `hosted_text_in` | Display: “Text generation input”
- Code: `hosted_text_out` | Display: “Text generation output”
- Code: `hosted_embed` | Display: “Embeddings”
- Code: `hosted_rerank` | Display: “Rerank”
- Code: `dedicated_gpu_hr` | Display: “Reserved GPU hours”
- Code: `dedicated_overage` | Display: “Overage usage” (if we support)
Add-ons (dimension code): `compliance_pack`, `premium_support`
All <= 32 chars.

Laura Bennett: “Overage usage” is too vague for finance. Could be interpreted as anything. Maybe “Usage overage”?

Soojin Lee: +1 to “Usage overage” (still generic but clearer).

Cole Summers: ok.

Naomi Feldman: “Compliance Pack” as an add-on dimension is fine. In security docs we’ll call it “Compliance documentation bundle” to be explicit.

Sofia Mendes: Please avoid “bundle” in legal docs unless it’s defined. But marketplace description can say “includes security documentation and enhanced audit log options” etc.

Stephanie Nguyen: Looks good. Chris—can you confirm with AWS/GCP partner folks that “Hosted API (Usage-based)” etc. fits their naming best practices?

Chris Osei: Will do. Also: AWS reviewers previously asked us to not use “API” too prominently in product title but plan name “Hosted API” is OK. I’ll validate.

Isabelle Laurent: Once confirmed, I’ll update HubSpot required fields + deal ops doc to use these exact strings.

Lukas Meyer: One more thing: for GCP, can we keep capitalization consistent (Title Case)? “Hosted API (Usage-based)” etc.

Soojin Lee: yes, Title Case.

Stephanie Nguyen: Locking this unless partner portals push back. Thanks all.

Stephanie Nguyen: AWS portal constraint: For SaaS Contract + metering, the "dimension names" are visible to buyers in the billing breakdown. If we put “hosted_payg_tokens” as the dimension name, that’s ugly.

Cole Summers: Yep. Suggest:
- Dimension *codes* short + stable
- Dimension *display names* buyer-friendly (AWS allows both; GCP has similar concept via SKU + metric label)
Example:
Code: `tokens_in_k` / Display: “Input tokens (per 1K)”
Code: `tokens_out_k` / Display: “Output tokens (per 1K)”
Code: `embeddings_k` / Display: “Embeddings (per 1K vectors)”

Soojin Lee: For GCP, we should avoid “per 1K vectors” if the official unit is per 1K requests or per 1K tokens. Need to match what our billing pipeline actually reports.

Cole Summers: Internally embeddings are billed per 1K input tokens equivalent today for most models. But marketplace wants a simple unit. If we label it “Embeddings (per 1K requests)” we have to meter requests, not tokens.

Chris Osei: Let’s not solve units here; thread is naming. But we should pick names that don’t lock us into the wrong unit.

Ben Carter: +1. Buyers mostly care that it’s understandable. "Embeddings units" is vague but safe.

Stephanie Nguyen: OK so for naming: keep dimensions generic enough to not misrepresent the unit.
Proposed dimension display names:
- “Text generation input”
- “Text generation output”
- “Embeddings”
- “Rerank requests”

Cole Summers: Works, and we can put exact units in the listing FAQ / pricing section.

Lukas Meyer: Back to plan names: can we standardize across AWS/GCP:
Hosted = “Hosted API (Usage-based)”
Dedicated = “Dedicated Capacity (Reserved)”
Private = “Private Deployment (VPC)”
Add-on = “Compliance Pack”

Soojin Lee: I’m ok with those. “Reserved” reads better than “Contract”. “Usage-based” is clear.

Laura Bennett: “Reserved” also maps to finance language (reserved capacity). Fine.

Sofia Mendes: “Compliance Pack” is ok if we’re careful in description: documentation, controls, audit log retention options, etc. But don’t imply certification.

Naomi Feldman: Suggest “Compliance Pack (Docs + Controls)” in the description, not the name.

Isabelle Laurent: CRM/ops note: whatever we choose should match HubSpot picklists (marketplace_sku). Please don’t rename after we roll out, it breaks reporting.

Chris Osei: Summarizing convention v2:
Listing product name: “Redwood Inference (LLM Inference Platform)”
Plan display names (AWS/GCP):
1) Hosted API (Usage-based)
2) Dedicated Capacity (Reserved)
3) Private Deployment (VPC)
Add-on display names:
- Compliance Pack
- Premium Support
Dimension display names: human-friendly service labels (no internal codes)
Dimension codes: stable snake_case matching internal meter family

Stephanie Nguyen: Any objections to “Private Deployment (VPC)” vs “Private (VPC)”?

Lukas Meyer: Prefer the longer one. Buyers understand “Private Deployment” better.

Soojin Lee: Agree.

Cole Summers: Fine for display. For internal slug we’ll use `private_vpc`.

Ben Carter: Can we ensure the console shows the display name + a short explanation (“You subscribed to: Private Deployment (VPC)”)? That reduces “what did I buy” tickets.

Chris Osei: Noted; that’s in onboarding UX workstream but we’ll feed this naming into Chloe’s team.

Stephanie Nguyen: Action items:
- Soojin: update copy guidelines to use these exact plan names.
- Cole: propose final dimension codes + display names list that won’t break billing catalog.
- Sofia/Naomi: sanity check “Compliance Pack” description language.
- Isabelle: align HubSpot picklists.
We’ll consider this “tentative approved” pending partner portal validation.

Cole Summers: I’ll post a mapping table here once I’ve checked against `billing_catalog.yaml` constraints (length, allowed chars).

Soojin Lee: :thumbsup:

Chris Osei: Dropping one more constraint from AWS: dimension *codes* can’t start with a number and should be <= 32 chars. Also avoid hyphens.

Cole Summers: good call.

(Next day)

Cole Summers: Draft dimension codes (stable) + display labels (buyer-facing):
- Code: `hosted_text_in` | Display: “Text generation input”
- Code: `hosted_text_out` | Display: “Text generation output”
- Code: `hosted_embed` | Display: “Embeddings”
- Code: `hosted_rerank` | Display: “Rerank”
- Code: `dedicated_gpu_hr` | Display: “Reserved GPU hours”
- Code: `dedicated_overage` | Display: “Overage usage” (if we support)
Add-ons (dimension code): `compliance_pack`, `premium_support`
All <= 32 chars.

Laura Bennett: “Overage usage” is too vague for finance. Could be interpreted as anything. Maybe “Usage overage”?

Soojin Lee: +1 to “Usage overage” (still generic but clearer).

Cole Summers: ok.

Naomi Feldman: “Compliance Pack” as an add-on dimension is fine. In security docs we’ll call it “Compliance documentation bundle” to be explicit.

Sofia Mendes: Please avoid “bundle” in legal docs unless it’s defined. But marketplace description can say “includes security documentation and enhanced audit log options” etc.

Stephanie Nguyen: Looks good. Chris—can you confirm with AWS/GCP partner folks that “Hosted API (Usage-based)” etc. fits their naming best practices?

Chris Osei: Will do. Also: AWS reviewers previously asked us to not use “API” too prominently in product title but plan name “Hosted API” is OK. I’ll validate.

Isabelle Laurent: Once confirmed, I’ll update HubSpot required fields + deal ops doc to use these exact strings.

Lukas Meyer: One more thing: for GCP, can we keep capitalization consistent (Title Case)? “Hosted API (Usage-based)” etc.

Soojin Lee: yes, Title Case.

Stephanie Nguyen: Locking this unless partner portals push back. Thanks all.

title: #partnerships: 1769368455
preview:
Chris Osei: AWS review feedback came back (round 1). Dropping the key items here + proposed owners. Please react :thumbsup: if you can take, or reply w/ an alt owner.

Chris Osei: Summary from AWS team:
1) Missing artifacts: need Security Overview doc (public URL), Data Flow Diagram (image/PDF), Support contact + SLA statement, and EULA/terms link.
2) Wording changes: remove/soften comparative claims (“best-in-class”, “#1 latency”) unless we cite a public benchmark. Also avoid “SOC2 certified” phrasing.
3) Offer structure: clarify what’s included in each SKU vs add-ons; current copy reads like Dedicated/Private are features inside Hosted.
4) Screenshots: they want at least 3 product screenshots (console, usage dashboard, audit logs) w/ captions.
5) Metering/billing: ensure the metering dimension names in the listing match what’s configured in the AWS metering integration (they flagged 2 dimension labels as inconsistent).

Stephanie Nguyen: Re #2, they highlighted the sentence “industry-leading latency across all regions” as problematic. We can say “optimized for low latency and high throughput” (no superlatives) and link to our perf page if we have one.

Soojin Lee: Yep. I’ll do a copy sweep today. If we don’t have a public benchmark URL we should delete the numeric latency claims too.

Jordan Blake: Agree. Don’t get cute w/ claims in marketplace. Keep it enterprise-safe.

Camila Reyes: On screenshots: I can get 3–4 captures from Console (model catalog + routing policy, usage/cost breakdown, audit log viewer). Need final IA/URLs for captions.

Alex Martinez: We can point AWS to the docs-site Security Overview page once PR-3422 lands. I’ll confirm the final URL + get redirects if needed (don’t want to change after submission).

Naomi Feldman: For security wording: please don’t say “SOC2 certified.” Preferred: “SOC 2 Type II report available under NDA/on request” + “controls aligned to ISO 27001” (only if we’re not implying certification). Also, AWS asked specifically for data retention defaults + audit log retention.

Chris Osei: Owners draft:
- Copy edits (remove comparative claims, clarify SKU boundaries): @Soojin Lee
- Security Overview + shared responsibility blurb + retention/audit statements: @Naomi Feldman (review w/ Dr. Aisha Rahman)
- Data Flow Diagram export (PDF) for submission: @Camila Reyes (source from Security/Eng if needed)
- Support + SLA language block + support contact email: @Ben Carter (loop CS)
- EULA/terms link + refund/credits language for marketplace: @Sofia Mendes
- Metering dimension label check (AWS listing ↔ internal meters): @Cole Summers
- Final docs URLs / redirects readiness: @Alex Martinez

Cole Summers: On #5: I know the mismatch they’re seeing. Our internal meter is `hosted_tokens` but the draft listing dimension says “Hosted - Tokens (Input/Output)”. AWS wants exact names. I can propose updated dimension strings that match what we’ll ship in the metering config.

Soojin Lee: Please send me the exact dimension strings you want in listing so I can align copy + the pricing table.

Cole Summers: Will do in ~1 hr. Also note: they flagged “Dedicated GPU Hours” dimension; we’re actually metering “Reserved Capacity Units” not literal GPU hours.

Sofia Mendes: For terms: AWS wants a publicly accessible EULA link. We can use our standard MSA/EULA page, but we need a marketplace addendum (billing via AWS, cancellation, refund policy). I’ll draft a short addendum and route to Michael Grant for sign-off.

Ben Carter: Support/SLA: for marketplace public listing, we should keep it simple: “Standard support via support@redwoodinference.com” + “Enterprise SLAs available for Dedicated/Private” (don’t state response times publicly unless we’re ready to commit).

Jordan Blake: +1. Don’t publish response times unless Legal + CS agree.

Camila Reyes: For the audit logs screenshot, do we have a customer-safe view w/ no PII? I can use staging with fake org.

Naomi Feldman: Use staging w/ synthetic data only. Also, AWS asked for “encryption at rest/in transit” statement. We can say TLS in transit + encryption at rest in cloud storage + KMS support for Private/VPC. Keep it generic (no algorithm name unless we’re consistent everywhere).

Alex Martinez: Re “public URL”: AWS reviewer specifically asked that the Security Overview not be behind a login wall. We can publish a high-level page + keep evidence/report links gated.

Chris Osei: Yep, that matches our publication plan.

Chris Osei: Direct quotes from AWS feedback (so we don’t lose nuance):
- “Please provide a customer-facing architecture or data flow diagram showing where customer prompts, outputs, and logs reside.”
- “Avoid comparative marketing claims unless supported by a publicly accessible citation.”
- “Clarify support contact and escalation process for enterprise customers.”
- “Dimension names in listing must match metering integration.”

Soojin Lee: Copy plan:
- Replace “industry-leading / best-in-class” w/ “optimized for”
- Remove specific latency numbers unless we link a public benchmark
- Split sections: Hosted API vs Dedicated vs Private with “Purchase via marketplace” bullets per SKU
- Add a short “How it works” (subscribe → link org → API key → usage + billing)

Chris Osei: Great. Camila—can you add captions that reinforce Hosted/Dedicated/Private difference?

Camila Reyes: Yep. I’ll draft captions and tag Soojin for review.

Naomi Feldman: For the security packet: we should ensure retention language is consistent:
- Prompt/response retention default (what we store + for how long)
- Configurable retention controls (if any)
- Audit logs retention
- “Customer data not used for training by default” (if we’re stating it publicly, wording must match existing policy)

Jordan Blake: Please keep “not used for training” statement consistent across all channels. Prospects ask about this constantly.

Chris Osei: Timeline: AWS said if we resubmit assets by EOW, we can stay in the current review window. Can owners target updates by Thursday 3pm PT?

Soojin Lee: I can get the copy edits + updated FAQ by tomorrow.

Cole Summers: Metering dimension names by today.

Sofia Mendes: Terms addendum draft by Thursday morning.

Alex Martinez: Security overview page URL + redirects by Thursday.

Camila Reyes: Screenshots + diagram export by Thursday (assuming we get the final data flow diagram from Eng/Security).

Naomi Feldman: I’ll provide the security wording + review the diagram by Wednesday.

Chris Osei: Action items (tracking here):
- @Cole Summers send canonical AWS dimension names + confirm Dedicated dimension semantics
- @Soojin Lee update AWS listing copy + SKU clarity + remove unsupported claims
- @Naomi Feldman provide marketplace-safe security language (SOC2 phrasing, encryption, retention, audit logs)
- @Camila Reyes capture 3–4 screenshots + export data flow diagram PDF
- @Alex Martinez confirm public docs URLs + redirect plan (no post-submit breakage)
- @Sofia Mendes draft EULA/marketplace addendum + refund/cancellation language
- @Ben Carter finalize support contact block

Chris Osei: Please reply in-thread if any of these are blocked. I’ll update the partner review tracker once we have the revised bundle.

title: #partnerships: 1768801120
preview:
Maya Hernandez: Heads up: Marketplace partner (Cloud Marketplace Partner team) is asking for our “standard security collateral” to attach to the listing + send to their review queue. They want it by EOW. What can we provide today + who owns each piece?

Ben Ito: Specifically they asked for:
- SOC 2 status/report
- ISO 27001 status
- encryption + key mgmt
- data residency + retention
- audit logs
- SSO/SAML/SCIM
- subprocessor list / DPA language

Ben Ito: Also: what’s public vs what needs NDA. They want a single “security overview” PDF if possible.

Camila Reyes: We should be careful w/ the ISO request. We can’t imply certified. We *can* say “aligned with ISO 27001 principles” if that’s the approved phrasing. @Olga Petrov can you confirm the exact line?

Olga Petrov: Yep. Approved language: “Redwood Inference maintains a SOC 2 Type II audited security program and aligns its security controls with commonly recognized frameworks (e.g., ISO/IEC 27001).” Do NOT say “ISO certified” / “ISO compliant.”

Marcus Lin: For the partner review: we can give them (1) the enterprise security overview (public-friendly), (2) SOC 2 report under NDA only, (3) policy statements re: encryption/logging/auth. No pen test report / vuln scan artifacts unless we go through evidence request process.

Daniel Carter: +1. SOC 2 report distribution is gated. Needs NDA on file + tracked request. We can share a short SOC2 one-pager publicly, but the report itself is restricted.

Maya Hernandez: Cool. Can we list what we’ll send + owners?

Markus Klein: From SE side, most common “marketplace security checklist” asks map to our enablement kit one-pagers:
- SOC 2 one-pager (public)
- ISO alignment one-pager (public)
- Data residency/retention one-pager (public, but needs the caveats)
- Encryption + KMS one-pager (public)
- Audit logging overview (public)
- SSO/SAML/SCIM + RBAC capabilities (public-ish / internal ref -> we should provide a customer-facing summary)

Fatima Noor: Data residency: we need to be explicit by deployment mode (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private). Partner reviewers get twitchy if we say “EU residency” without clarifying what data (prompts/logs) + what controls exist.

Elliot Price: For docs links, we can point them at the public “Enterprise Security Overview” page (in progress) + individual pages for audit logging + retention/residency. I can package it as a single link bundle.

Kira Thompson: If they really want a PDF “security overview,” we can export the Confluence page once finalized, but we should make sure it’s using the approved messaging pillars.

Soojin Lee: I’ll align the narrative so it’s partner-safe + consistent w/ Product Marketing. We should avoid anything that reads like “guarantees” (esp. SLAs) unless it’s contractually tied to Dedicated.

Camila Reyes: Proposed deliverables to send partner:
1) Security & Trust Overview (1-pager) — owner: @Soojin Lee (messaging) + @Camila Reyes (final check)
2) SOC 2 one-pager — owner: @Olga Petrov / @Daniel Carter (GRC) (public)
3) ISO alignment one-pager — owner: @Olga Petrov (public)
4) Data residency + retention one-pager — owner: @Fatima Noor (content) + @Camila Reyes (wording)
5) Encryption + KMS one-pager — owner: @Marcus Lin (tech review)
6) Audit logging overview — owner: @Marcus Lin (tech) + @Elliot Price (docs link)
7) SSO/SAML/SCIM + RBAC summary — owner: @Naomi Feldman (capability alignment) + @Markus Klein (SE readability)

Camila Reyes: Anything involving SOC2 report / pen test reports: respond with “available under NDA via evidence request process” + link.

Naomi Feldman: For SSO/SCIM/RBAC: we support SAML SSO + SCIM for provisioning for enterprise plans, RBAC in console and API. We need to avoid promising “custom roles” unless we’re sure it’s GA. I’ll pull the approved snippet.

Marcus Lin: Encryption/KMS: we can say TLS in transit, encryption at rest, managed keys by default, and for Private deployment we support customer-managed keys via KMS/HSM integrations depending on environment. Let’s keep it “options available” and tie to deployment mode.

Ben Ito: Partner asked if we do “no training on customer data.” Do we have a clean sentence for that?

Olga Petrov: Use the approved definition: “We do not use customer prompts or outputs submitted to the Redwood Inference service to train or fine-tune foundation models.” Don’t broaden it to “never access” etc.

Daniel Carter: Also call out: access is restricted + logged for support purposes, per least privilege. (But don’t overdo it in partner collateral.)

Maya Hernandez: Timeline: they want by Friday 3pm PT. Can we have drafts by Thurs noon?

Soojin Lee: I can have a first pass of the Security & Trust overview by tomorrow EOD.

Elliot Price: I’ll compile the canonical links + confirm what’s public. If the public docs page isn’t live yet, I’ll provide a “temporary Confluence export” that’s safe to share.

Kira Thompson: If we’re sharing Confluence exports externally, please mark “External share OK” in the footer + ensure no internal-only sections.

Camila Reyes: I’ll create a quick checklist for the partner packet:
- No roadmap / no ISO cert claims
- Deployment-mode caveats included
- SOC 2 report gated (NDA)
- Use “request evidence” link for restricted artifacts

Ben Ito: :thumbsup: I’ll reply to partner: “We’ll send a security collateral bundle incl. overview + one-pagers; SOC2 report available under NDA via process.”

Slackbot: Reminder set by Maya Hernandez for Thursday, 12:00 PM: “Marketplace partner security collateral: drafts due.”

Olga Petrov: Use the approved definition: “We do not use customer prompts or outputs submitted to the Redwood Inference service to train or fine-tune foundation models.” Don’t broaden it to “never access” etc.

Daniel Carter: Also call out: access is restricted + logged for support purposes, per least privilege. (But don’t overdo it in partner collateral.)

Maya Hernandez: Timeline: they want by Friday 3pm PT. Can we have drafts by Thurs noon?

Soojin Lee: I can have a first pass of the Security & Trust overview by tomorrow EOD.

Elliot Price: I’ll compile the canonical links + confirm what’s public. If the public docs page isn’t live yet, I’ll provide a “temporary Confluence export” that’s safe to share.

Kira Thompson: If we’re sharing Confluence exports externally, please mark “External share OK” in the footer + ensure no internal-only sections.

Camila Reyes: I’ll create a quick checklist for the partner packet:
- No roadmap / no ISO cert claims
- Deployment-mode caveats included
- SOC 2 report gated (NDA)
- Use “request evidence” link for restricted artifacts

Ben Ito: :thumbsup: I’ll reply to partner: “We’ll send a security collateral bundle incl. overview + one-pagers; SOC2 report available under NDA via process.”

Slackbot: Reminder set by Maya Hernandez for Thursday, 12:00 PM: “Marketplace partner security collateral: drafts due.”

title: #partnerships: 1768535122
preview:
Maya Patel: Quick q for growth folks — we’re lining up a Feb partner push w/ Cloud marketplaces (AWS + GCP listings) + 1 co-marketed webinar. If we’re running retargeting at the same time, are we coordinating audiences/UTMs so attribution doesn’t get messy?

Ben Carter: yeah good catch. Right now retargeting plan is (1) site visitors, (2) signup-no-activation, (3) activated-no-return. If partner traffic is hitting the same landing pages, we’ll end up with mixed UTMs + multi-touch confusion.

Tyler Benson: +1. We need to decide: (a) isolate partner landing pages (no retargeting pixel audiences off them), or (b) keep pixels but enforce strict UTM taxonomy + attribution rules (first-touch vs last-touch) so we can still measure.

Maya Patel: partner team preference is to keep partner reporting clean (they’ll want “influenced pipeline” for marketplace + webinar). But I also don’t want to kneecap retargeting if it’s our best activation lever.

Ben Carter: we can do a compromise:
- Dedicated partner LPs: /lp/aws-marketplace, /lp/gcp-marketplace, /lp/partner-webinar
- Retargeting audiences exclude visitors whose last session had `utm_medium=partner` or `utm_source` in {aws_marketplace, gcp_marketplace, partner_webinar}
- Keep generic homepage/docs traffic eligible for retargeting

Tyler Benson: that works. Also: if the partner campaigns are driving *new* signups, they’ll fall into “signup-no-activation” audience unless we suppress. Do we want to suppress them too?

Maya Patel: I think yes for the first 2 weeks? Otherwise partner will claim conversions but retargeting will “close the loop” and get last-touch.

Jordan Blake: from Sales side: please don’t retarget accounts that are coming in through a marketplace deal motion if we’re already engaging them. Similar to the “active opp exclusion” rule. We can treat marketplace-sourced inbound as “sales assisted potential” and suppress from generic ads.

Ben Carter: ok. so:
1) Suppress any domain on active opp list (already in plan)
2) Add suppression for partner/mktplace-sourced signups for N days
Question is N = 14? 30?

Tyler Benson: I’d do 30-day suppression for partner-sourced signups for retargeting *ads* (email lifecycle still ok). That gives partner attribution time + avoids annoying them with ads right after a webinar.

Isabelle Laurent: I can help implement a “partner_sourced” flag in HubSpot / data export so we can build the suppression list. Need a clear definition of partner-sourced:
- any session w/ `utm_medium=partner` OR
- landing page path starts with `/lp/aws-marketplace` etc OR
- referrer matches marketplace domains?

Tyler Benson: define it via UTMs + LP path. Referrers are unreliable. For marketplaces we should force UTMs in the listing CTA and any co-marketing emails.

Maya Patel: we can enforce UTMs in partner assets. For AWS marketplace listing, the CTA link is configurable — we’ll set:
`utm_source=aws_marketplace&utm_medium=partner&utm_campaign=q1_2026_marketplace_push&utm_content=listing_cta`
Similarly for GCP.

Ben Carter: cool. For webinar, we’ll set `utm_source=partner_webinar&utm_medium=partner...`.

Tyler Benson: also we should add a separate retargeting campaign bucket for partner traffic *later*, so we can re-engage them with partner-aligned creative (e.g., “deploy in your VPC / dedicated capacity”) without contaminating the main lifecycle experiments readout.

Camila Reyes: agree w/ separation. Guardrail: the sprint goal is activation lift for Hosted API self-serve. Partner/marketplace is a different motion. Keep reporting separate so we don’t declare victory based on partner-driven intent.

Ben Carter: :thumbsup: action items:
- Maya: confirm partner LPs + UTM conventions with partners
- Tyler: update retargeting-audience-definitions doc w/ partner suppression rules + 30-day window
- Isabelle: confirm we can export “partner_sourced” list weekly for ad platform exclusions
- Ben: update sprint brief + measurement notes to explicitly exclude partner campaigns from primary experiment attribution

Jordan Blake: can you also add “marketplace inbound” to the exclusion category we review weekly? Similar to enterprise opp suppression.

Tyler Benson: yes. We’ll add a weekly sync line item: “new partner-sourced domains + marketplace inbound → exclude from retargeting.”

Maya Patel: perfect. I’ll drop the list of planned partner pushes + dates in this thread once final.

Ben Carter: thanks for catching this early. Would’ve been a mess in Week 3 readout otherwise.

Tyler Benson: +1. Also reminder: for the ad platforms, we need consistent `utm_campaign` prefixes. Propose:
- `lgx_ret_...` for lifecycle growth retargeting
- `partner_...` for partner pushes
Then we can filter cleanly in dashboards.

Maya Patel: Works. I’ll align w/ Partnerships comms team.

Isabelle Laurent: ping me when UTM strings are final; I’ll update the HubSpot tracking rules + property mapping.

Jordan Blake: :eyes: on the suppression list implementation. Don’t want any “active opp” seeing generic signup ads.

Ben Carter: will do. Closing loop once Tyler updates the doc + I push the change to the retargeting ticket.

title: #partnerships: 1767011111
preview:
Rina Gomez: quick callout 1: marketplace leads keep slipping between our SI partners and field reps. need a short "claim" guideline.
Ethan Park: yep seen a few duplicates this week. simplest is: claim in CRM -> partners-bot confirmation -> 48h hold by claimer.
Nora Walsh: 48h hold seems fair. add requirement: claimer must add brief reason + expected next step (demo, intro, qualify).
Rina Gomez: propose formalizing: 1) SI clicks claim -> record shows SI name, timestamp, reason, next step. 2) partners-bot posts a confirmation in #partners with lead id and claimer. 3) field rep is CC'd if account is field-owned.
Ethan Park: Q: what about overlapping claims from multiple SIs? first-claim wins, others get notified and put on watcher list.
Nora Walsh: also add mapping check: if CRM has SI_primary flag, the primary SI gets notified and gets 24h exclusive right to claim before open to others.
partners-bot: [automated] Draft rule saved: claim flow v0.2.
Rina Gomez: temp legal note: include short consent on sharing contact details with SI when claim is created. Samir from legal will review.
Ethan Park: small UX note: make claim button visible on marketplace lead card and on CRM widget. low-friction.
Nora Walsh: metrics we should track: claim rate, duplicate claims, time to first contact, drop-offs after claim.
Rina Gomez: next steps: Rina to add claim UI mock + PR; Ethan to wire webhook for claim -> partners-bot; Nora to draft metrics dashboard. Pilot with Horizon SI next week.
partners-bot: [automated] Actions created: UI PR assigned to Rina (due Wed), webhook task to Ethan, metrics ticket to Nora.
Rina Gomez: thanks all, keeping it light so SIs actually use it.

title: #partnerships: 1760265020
preview:
Jordan Blake: tossing an idea in here — can we tie the first public case study drop to a joint webinar w/ a partner?

Two angles:
1) cloud marketplace partner (listing + “how to buy Redwood via marketplace + Dedicated/Private options”)
2) SIEM integration partner (security story: audit logs -> SIEM, SOC workflows)

Feels like this could help enterprise pipeline if we time it w/ the launch. Anyone already talking to partner marketing on either?

Camila Reyes: yes please. we’re trying to stack distribution on top of the case study launch anyway.

q: which customer story are you thinking? Northwind? Helios? (Helios would map cleaner to SIEM imo but approvals are… spicy)

Jordan Blake: exactly. SIEM angle probably maps to Helios (VPC + audit logs). marketplace angle maps to Northwind (Dedicated, predictable spend) or even Atlas if they want procurement-y messaging.

Soojin Lee: +1 but we need to be careful on claims.
- for SIEM: we can say “audit log export + integrations” but not imply out-of-the-box connectors unless we actually have them / partner confirmed.
- for marketplace: avoid “1-click deploy everywhere” style language.

If we do webinar, I’d like to own the abstract + talk track so it stays in our approved pillars.

Markus Klein: from SE side: I can do a “what the architecture looks like” segment (high level). but if Helios is involved we should expect them to request anonymization / no detailed network diagrams.

Also: partner will want to show an integration diagram. we can provide a sanitized version.

Tyler Benson: operationally this is doable if we pick a date soon. i’ll need:
- partner name + co-marketing terms (are they emailing to their list? are we getting lead share?)
- landing page owner (webinar reg)
- UTM plan so we can attribute to pipeline influence

Isabelle Laurent: +1 on lead share. if partner is generating registrations, we need fields + routing rules. if we don’t get emails back we can only count “influence” not sourced.

Avery Johnson: selfishly: marketplace angle could be nice for a couple deals in-flight where procurement is dragging. “we’re on marketplace” is an unlock.

But if we do SIEM angle, that’s a strong late-stage trust builder too (security teams love seeing workflows).

Rachel Kim: quick CS flag: if we’re involving a customer speaker, we need to limit load + set expectations (1 rehearsal + 1 live + 1 optional recording review). we’ve already been cautious about not over-asking.

Miguel Santos: agree w/ Rachel. also: if Helios, their compliance team will want to see slides in advance and will likely request to remove any hard metrics.

Stephanie Nguyen: from sales leadership POV, a webinar can work IF:
1) we can re-use recording in outbound + late-stage sequences
2) we have a clear CTA (talk to sales about Dedicated/Private)
3) it doesn’t slip the case study timeline

Jordan Blake: makes sense.

On partner side, I can get intros w/:
- “CloudPort Marketplace” (our marketplace channel contact) — they’ve been asking for a customer story tie-in
- “SentinelForge” (SIEM partner) — they’ve floated co-marketing around audit log integrations

No promises on lead share yet.

Camila Reyes: for timeline: our target is to publish first story late in the push (week 7–9). if we do webinar, I’d rather schedule it ~1 week after publish so we can point to the live page.

Also: customer consent needs to explicitly cover co-marketing + partner logo appearing alongside (or at least “partner name mentioned”). Legal will ask.

Soojin Lee: +1000. please loop Michael Grant/Sofia Mendes once we pick the option. partner co-branding has weird edge cases.

Markus Klein: Q: do we actually have a named “SIEM integration” today or is it more like “webhook export / S3 export that customers forward to SIEM”?

Don’t want to overstate.

Jordan Blake: mostly the latter. SentinelForge has an ingestion recipe; they want to present it as “supported pattern.”

Soojin Lee: then we should say “validated integration pattern” / “reference architecture” not “native integration.”

Tyler Benson: if we go marketplace angle, who’s the customer? Northwind seems easiest given we already have momentum there.

Camila Reyes: Northwind is the most likely to go public first. Helios is still negotiating compliance redlines.

Maybe: webinar = marketplace + Northwind (safer). then later we do a security-focused session once Helios is cleared.

Avery Johnson: that’s my preference. keep first one simple.

Miguel Santos: Northwind is generally cooperative but they’re picky about logo placement + review windows. if partner logo is on the same slide/page, they’ll want to approve.

Rachel Kim: and we should confirm they’re ok being positioned adjacent to the marketplace partner (some customers hate looking like they’re “endorsing” another vendor).

Jordan Blake: ok.

Action proposal:
1) I’ll ask CloudPort Marketplace for a co-marketing brief: what they’ll do (email, social, event listing), what we get back (lead list or not), date options.
2) Camila/Miguel: can you sanity check w/ Northwind if they’d be open to a webinar *in principle* (no commitment) + whether they’re ok with co-branding w/ marketplace partner?
3) Soojin: draft a 3-sentence abstract + 3 proof points we can safely claim.
4) Tyler/Isabelle: define required form fields + UTM + HubSpot tagging (so we can report).

Camila Reyes: :thumbsup: works. ping me when you have partner constraints so we can slot into distribution calendar.

Soojin Lee: I’ll draft something today. will keep it to performance economics + predictable capacity + procurement friction reduction (via marketplace) and avoid deep metrics.

Markus Klein: if Northwind is on, I can present a “Dedicated deployment overview” without showing exact sizing. I’ll also prep a safe architecture slide we can reuse on the case study page.

Tyler Benson: re: tracking — please ask partner if they can include UTMs on their promo links. if not, we’ll have to use a separate “partner invite” reg link.

Isabelle Laurent: and confirm if they require using *their* registration platform. if so, we need API export or at least CSV within 24h.

Stephanie Nguyen: also add a slide at end for “how to evaluate Redwood Dedicated/Private” + who to contact. we should route inbound to AEs/SDRs properly.

Jordan Blake: got it. I’ll update thread once I hear back from CloudPort.

Camila Reyes: thanks. keeping this thread as our decision log so it doesn’t get scattered.

Jordan Blake: quick update — CloudPort is open to co-hosting, but they want:
- 45-min webinar + 15-min Q&A
- them on for 10 min to cover marketplace purchase flow
- they *can* send to their enterprise list but lead share is “opt-in” checkbox only

They asked for 2–3 date options in ~3–4 weeks.

Tyler Benson: opt-in checkbox lead share is fine. we should make it explicit on the form (“share my info with CloudPort + Redwood”).

Isabelle Laurent: yep. as long as we get the opt-in field in the export.

Soojin Lee: if they’re on-stage for purchase flow, we need to ensure it doesn’t become a pure procurement webinar. we should keep it anchored to the case study story + outcomes.

Camila Reyes: agree. let’s treat CloudPort segment as “how to streamline vendor onboarding” not “buy now.”

Miguel Santos: i’ll check with Northwind on comfort level w/ partner present + recorded session.

Rachel Kim: pls also confirm Northwind speaker capacity. one person is better than multiple.

Markus Klein: I can cover the technical part if Northwind wants to stay lighter. (e.g., Northwind does 5 min context + quote, not a full technical deep dive)

Avery Johnson: +1. if customer is nervous, we can keep them to “why we chose Redwood + what improved” and have Markus do architecture.

Jordan Blake: ok. i’ll hold dates until we hear from Northwind.

Camila Reyes: if Northwind says yes, we’ll lock dates + start the approval checklist (slides > customer review > legal > partner review). keep me posted.
