Rachel Kim: pls also confirm Northwind speaker capacity. one person is better than multiple.

Markus Klein: I can cover the technical part if Northwind wants to stay lighter. (e.g., Northwind does 5 min context + quote, not a full technical deep dive)

Avery Johnson: +1. if customer is nervous, we can keep them to “why we chose Redwood + what improved” and have Markus do architecture.

Jordan Blake: ok. i’ll hold dates until we hear from Northwind.

Camila Reyes: if Northwind says yes, we’ll lock dates + start the approval checklist (slides > customer review > legal > partner review). keep me posted.

title: #partnerships: 1749240099
preview:
Noura El-Khoury: Kicking off partner amplification planning for the benchmark report launch. We have Cloud marketplace + newsletter options with 2 partners (CloudCo + VectorDBCo). Need to align on: (1) embargo date/time, (2) approved shared messaging, (3) exact assets we can hand over (landing page URL, PDF, hero charts), (4) UTM/tagging.

Soojin Lee: thx. Tentative public launch is Tue 7/15 9am PT (pending final legal sign-off + docs deploy). Can we hold partners to that embargo? Also want to avoid “#1 fastest” type phrasing.

Ben Carter: +1. For growth: we want each partner channel to drive to the interactive page w/ UTMs + a secondary CTA to “Start with credits” + “Talk to sales” for Dedicated/Private.

Tyler Benson: Re UTMs: can do `utm_source=cloudco&utm_medium=partner&utm_campaign=benchmark_report_2025` and separate ones for newsletter vs marketplace listing. Also we should align on one HubSpot campaign tag name so attribution isn’t a mess.

Camila Reyes: We should assume partners will want a quote or a “why this matters” blurb. We can provide a generic Redwood quote, but no comparative claims unless substantiated + in the approved language.

Wei Chen: Assets we’ll have by then: landing page, methodology summary, and the “How we measured” section. PDF will be final closer to launch week. If partners need a preview, we can share a static screenshot set under embargo.

Noura El-Khoury: For CloudCo, their marketplace team asked for: (a) 150–200 word listing update, (b) one image, (c) 3 bullet “customer benefits,” (d) link to a blog post. They also offered a newsletter slot (Thu 7/17) if we give them copy by 7/10.

Soojin Lee: Newsletter on 7/17 is fine as “post-launch” amplification. Marketplace update should go live same day as launch or after (not before).

Ben Carter: Agree. Also: please ensure partner copy doesn’t mention specific competitor names. We should keep it “production inference economics, measured across regions, reproducible methodology.”

Michael Grant: Legal note: partner co-marketing counts as “public claims.” Anything that includes performance comparisons needs: (1) our exact approved benchmark language, (2) the disclaimers, and (3) a link to methodology. No “best,” “fastest,” “cheapest,” “industry-leading,” etc. If you keep it descriptive + include the caveats, much easier.

Noura El-Khoury: Got it. I’ll send partners a “safe language pack” + required disclaimers block. Soojin/Wei can you share the latest approved short disclaimer text? (the one that fits in newsletters)

Wei Chen: Draft short disclaimer we’ve been using in docs PR reviews:
“Results reflect Redwood Inference measurements on a defined production-mix workload and may vary by prompt, traffic patterns, model version, and region capacity. See methodology for full configuration and limitations.”
We can shorten further if needed but should keep the “may vary” + methodology link.

Soojin Lee: + add: “pricing assumptions described in report” (since latency/$ depends on token pricing + instance attribution). Legal preferred wording was “see report for pricing inputs.”

Tyler Benson: For partner links, please use the landing page canonical URL (no docsite preview). I’ll generate UTM links once we confirm final slug. Also: for the marketplace listing, we should add a separate CTA link that goes to Dedicated sizing form (that’s how we’ve been routing enterprise interest).

Ben Carter: Do we have the slug? I’ve been seeing `.../benchmarks/latency-per-dollar` in drafts.

Wei Chen: Current working slug in docs PR is `/benchmarks/latency-per-dollar` but not final until web merges. I’ll confirm by EOD tomorrow.

Camila Reyes: Message framing for partners:
- Headline: “New benchmark report: latency/$ across models + regions (with reproducible methodology)”
- Why it matters: “Serving is where economics are won/lost; consistency across regions matters for real apps.”
- CTA: “Explore interactive tables + download PDF.”
No numbers in partner copy unless we are comfortable with them being taken out of context.

Soojin Lee: yep. Partners always try to pull a single stat. We should give them a “safe stat” that’s framed correctly or give none.

Noura El-Khoury: CloudCo specifically asked if they can say “up to X% better latency per dollar.” I told them not yet.

Michael Grant: Correct. “Up to” claims are high risk + often read as typical. If we do any % claims, we need the exact backing data + clear qualifiers. Recommend we avoid in partner channels.

Ben Carter: Alternative: give them a qualitative “Designed for production: lower latency and better unit economics via batching/caching/routing” + point to the interactive table for details.

Noura El-Khoury: For VectorDBCo: they offered a joint webinar mention + a short blurb in their monthly dev newsletter. They want an embargoed copy deck by 7/08.

Soojin Lee: Webinar mention ok, but the benchmark launch webinar is our thing (7/16). We can give them a “partner slide” with: (a) link to report, (b) use case: RAG latency with embeddings+rereank+gen (no performance claims), (c) how to try.

Wei Chen: I can make a partner-friendly “what’s inside the report” slide (no charts). If they insist on charts, use the ones with the big disclaimer footer.

Tyler Benson: If a partner is emailing their list, we need to ensure the landing page can handle the spike. Please coordinate the send time w/ Eng? (Even just “9am PT vs 12pm PT”).

Camila Reyes: +1 and we should avoid stacking partner sends on top of our own email send. Ben what’s our planned lifecycle blast time?

Ben Carter: PLG email is planned for launch day 9:15am PT. Enterprise nurture goes out 11am PT. If CloudCo newsletter is 7/17, we’re fine. For marketplace update, no “send,” just listing change.

Noura El-Khoury: Action items:
1) Noura: send partners embargo terms + safe language pack + asset list (by Fri 6/27)
2) Wei: confirm final landing page slug + provide short+long disclaimers (by EOD tomorrow)
3) Tyler: draft UTM scheme + HubSpot campaign tag naming + share with Noura (by Mon)
4) Soojin: confirm launch time + publish readiness gate w/ Legal (by Tue)
5) Michael: share the “disallowed phrases” snippet we can paste into partner comms (by Tue)
Sound right?

Soojin Lee: looks right. Please add “partners must link methodology” as a hard requirement.

Michael Grant: I’ll send you the exact disallowed phrasing list + required disclaimer components. Also include: “Do not publish screenshots of raw tables without the disclaimer visible.”

Wei Chen: Noted. I’ll package disclaimers + a “how to reference the report” paragraph.

Tyler Benson: Re campaign tag, proposing: `GTM_2025_BenchmarkReport_Launch`. Works across HubSpot + web analytics.

Ben Carter: Works. Also can we request partners use our shortened tracked links? Easier to QA.

Noura El-Khoury: yes, will do. I’ll also ask CloudCo if they can pin the marketplace update for 1 week.

Camila Reyes: Please make sure the partner-facing copy doesn’t imply endorsement (“CloudCo validates…” etc.). Keep it “Redwood published a report; available on CloudCo marketplace.”

Noura El-Khoury: :thumbsup: will keep it clean.

Soojin Lee: One more thing: if partners ask “which regions,” keep it aligned with the report (no promising regions we didn’t test). We’ve had confusion on “APAC coverage” before.

Wei Chen: I’ll include a line: “See report for tested regions; availability varies by product tier (Hosted/Dedicated/Private).”

Noura El-Khoury: perfect. I’ll follow up with partner contacts and drop any redlines/questions back here.

title: #partnerships: 1747601234
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — CloudPartner asked if we can lower first-year commit floor for private offers to $100k for SI-led deals. Thoughts?
Noah Patel: That would help close pilots faster. What's the risk on capacity/discounting?
Priya Singh: If we drop the floor we need to cap discount depth. Suggest 10% max first-year, with a 12-month renewal hurdle to re-evaluate.
Liam O'Neil: Also ask the SI to co-fund onboarding credits. If SI chips in $10k, we can relax floor to 75k.
Ava Chen: Good. Can we formalize as a rule: floor tiers [75k, 100k, 250k, 500k] => max discounts [6%, 8%, 12%, 18%] and SI kickers?
Priya Singh: I'm for tiers but keep a "no double-dipping" clause with marketplace promotional credits — credits shouldn't stack with seller rebates.
Noah Patel: Ops note: Dedicated capacity reservations need ~2 weeks lead time. Smaller floors might create fragmentation and hurt utilization.
Marketplace Bot: New private-offer template created: https://redwood.internal/templates/private-offer-v3 — includes fields for SI credit and HW bundle gating.
Ava Chen: Draft snippet for legal/PMM review:
```
Floor: $100,000
Max discount (year 1): 10% (non-stackable)
SI Co-sell credit: up to $10,000 one-time (applies only with certified SI)
HW bundle gating: eligible when HW spend >= $50,000
Renewal condition: Year 2 commit must be >= Year 1 actual + 5% to retain discount
Conversion window: 90 days from trial start to convert to committed private offer
```
Liam O'Neil: Add a clause that hardware partners must confirm supply SLA to qualify for HW gating.
Priya Singh: Also recommend a reporting field in the PO so we can track ARR uplift vs discount dollars per partner.
Noah Patel: I'll run a quick model on utilization and margin hit for the 75k and 100k floors, have numbers by EOD.
Ava Chen: Thanks — if Noah flags material margin risk we revert to 250k floor or require SI onboarding funding. Otherwise I'll take this to GTM sync tomorrow. :thumbsup:

title: #partnerships: 1746901202
preview:
Camila Reyes: pulling together options for reverse ETL / enrichment to get PLG signals + account context into HubSpot without us building a million one-off syncs. What vendors should we evaluate + do we even want a partner here vs build in-house?

Ben Carter: +1. Context: MVP is telemetry -> analytics -> scoring svc -> HubSpot API. Works, but we keep getting asks for “enrich with firmographic / intent / billing tier / workspace rollups” and it’s turning into a mini-ETL project.

Tyler Benson: from Marketing Ops POV: reverse ETL vendor would help us keep HubSpot properties sane + manage workflows/lists. But I’m nervous about another system writing to HubSpot without clear ownership + suppression logic.

David Kim: fwiw HubSpot API limits are real (already seeing 429s). If a vendor blasts updates, we’ll be back in rate-limit hell unless we design around it.

Isabelle Laurent: also need deterministic ownership rules. If vendor updates company fields it can accidentally trigger workflow reassignment. :grimacing:

Camila Reyes: vendors I’m hearing in the wild: Hightouch, Census, RudderStack (Reverse ETL), Segment (profiles), Tray/Workato (iPaaS-ish). For enrichment: Clearbit, Apollo, ZoomInfo, People Data Labs, 6sense (but $$$ / heavy).

Ben Carter: what’s the actual gap we’re solving?
1) reverse ETL: warehouse -> HubSpot property updates reliably (idempotent, rate-limited, retries, audit logs)
2) enrichment: append company size/industry + maybe domains + region
3) “intent” enrichment: web visits / doc views / etc (we already have docs instrumentation via @Elliot Price)

Logan Wright: engineering angle: we already have a scoring service; adding a queue + batcher for HubSpot writes is not crazy. Reverse ETL vendor mainly helps for *ongoing* property sync + UI for ops, plus connectors to enrichment sources.

Priya Natarajan: agreed. If we can keep the write-path controlled (our router service), I’d rather not introduce a second writer unless we can lock it down.

Tyler Benson: if we use a vendor, can we constrain them to only write to a limited set of properties? e.g. “enrichment_*” properties and leave “signal_*” + ownership fields to our service.

David Kim: yes, but HubSpot permissions are coarse. We can do it via conventions + workflow guardrails, not true per-property auth. We can also use separate private app tokens/accounts, but it’s still same portal.

Isabelle Laurent: +1 to limiting blast radius. Also any vendor needs to respect our existing owner preservation rules (don’t auto-enroll companies that already have an AE/CSM owner).

Camila Reyes: ok so sounds like two tracks:
A) reverse ETL vendor for enrichment-only fields + lists
B) keep signal routing + task creation in-house

Ben Carter: that seems right. Then we can evaluate vendors on:
- rate limit handling / batch semantics
- dedupe + idempotency (we do upserts by domain/org_id)
- audit trail + replay
- security posture (SOC2, data retention)
- ability to run on limited properties

Dr. Aisha Rahman: jumping in w/ security lens: any vendor touching customer usage-derived data in a 3rd party needs a clear DPA + documented retention + access controls. For PLG signals, we’re trying to be privacy-minimal. Prefer: vendor only gets company identifiers + firmographics, not raw usage.

Ben Carter: good call. We can keep raw usage + signal scoring internal, and only sync “signal summary” fields into HubSpot.

Elliot Price: for intent, we already have docs events piped into the analytics dataset. I’d rather not add a 3rd party tracker that creates a separate intent source of truth.

Camila Reyes: re vendors, I already have a warm intro to Hightouch (they’re pitching “reverse ETL + enrichment connectors”). Census is similar. RudderStack is more event pipeline + reverse ETL but could be heavier.

Tyler Benson: Hightouch/Census both have pretty strong HubSpot connectors + can manage computed traits in warehouse. I like that it’s easier for ops to tweak without eng deploys.

Logan Wright: but if they compute traits, we need to define the canonical logic. Otherwise we’ll have “meeting_booked_last_30d” defined three different ways.

David Kim: also please no one turns on “two-way sync” by accident. :sweat_smile:

Isabelle Laurent: +1. We should write down “single source of truth” rules:
- signal scoring + qualification: internal service
- enrichment fields: vendor
- ownership + task creation: internal service + HubSpot workflows

Camila Reyes: does anyone have strong preference between Hightouch vs Census?

Ben Carter: no strong preference. Hightouch has more partnerships momentum + I saw they have better rate-limit knobs. Census tends to be a bit simpler. We should run 2 short eval calls.

Tyler Benson: agree. Also ask both about:
- sandbox/test HubSpot portal support
- change management (preview diffs, dry-run)
- alerting when sync fails

Dr. Aisha Rahman: add: where do they store data? can they do EU data residency? and can we configure retention to <30d for any identifiers.

Priya Natarajan: and how they handle retries/backoff for 429s specifically (HubSpot has annoying rolling windows).

Camila Reyes: action items / next steps:
1) I’ll schedule vendor intros: Hightouch + Census (30 min each). Target next week.
2) @David Kim can you share our current HubSpot API rate limits + what endpoints we’re hitting most? (so we can ask pointed questions)
3) @Tyler Benson + @Isabelle Laurent can you list the exact HubSpot properties/workflows that are “hands off” / sensitive (ownership, lifecycle stage, etc.)
4) @Dr. Aisha Rahman can we get a quick checklist for vendor security review (DPA, SOC2, retention, sub-processors)?

David Kim: yep. I’ll drop notes here later today + a link to the internal rate-limit incident ticket.

Tyler Benson: I’ll put together a “do not touch” list + the properties we’d *want* a vendor to populate (industry, employee band, HQ region, technographics maybe).

Isabelle Laurent: I’ll add the routing/ownership gotchas + which workflows can accidentally reassign.

Ben Carter: also, can we define what “success” is for a vendor?
- reduce eng time spent on enrichment by X hrs/week
- keep signal task creation success rate >99%
- no increase in complaint rate (unwanted outreach) due to bad enrichment

Camila Reyes: :thumbsup: will add to the eval doc.

Camila Reyes: scheduling: proposing Tue 11am PT for Hightouch, Thu 10am PT for Census. Anyone want to join live? (partnerships + ops + security would be good)

Dr. Aisha Rahman: I can do Thu.

David Kim: Tue works.

Tyler Benson: both work.

Ben Carter: I’ll join Tue.

Camila Reyes: cool I’ll send cal invites + add agenda/questions in the invite.

David Kim: (reaction) :white_check_mark:

Priya Natarajan: if the vendor route looks messy, we should be ready with a “build it ourselves” fallback: batcher + queue + suppression rules + minimal enrichment via our own lookup (domain -> region).

Camila Reyes: agreed. We’ll treat vendor as phase 2, not a blocker for signal routing MVP.

Tyler Benson: Hightouch/Census both have pretty strong HubSpot connectors + can manage computed traits in warehouse. I like that it’s easier for ops to tweak without eng deploys.

Logan Wright: but if they compute traits, we need to define the canonical logic. Otherwise we’ll have “meeting_booked_last_30d” defined three different ways.

David Kim: also please no one turns on “two-way sync” by accident. :sweat_smile:

Isabelle Laurent: +1. We should write down “single source of truth” rules:
- signal scoring + qualification: internal service
- enrichment fields: vendor
- ownership + task creation: internal service + HubSpot workflows

Camila Reyes: does anyone have strong preference between Hightouch vs Census?

Ben Carter: no strong preference. Hightouch has more partnerships momentum + I saw they have better rate-limit knobs. Census tends to be a bit simpler. We should run 2 short eval calls.

Tyler Benson: agree. Also ask both about:
- sandbox/test HubSpot portal support
- change management (preview diffs, dry-run)
- alerting when sync fails

Dr. Aisha Rahman: add: where do they store data? can they do EU data residency? and can we configure retention to <30d for any identifiers.

Priya Natarajan: and how they handle retries/backoff for 429s specifically (HubSpot has annoying rolling windows).

Camila Reyes: action items / next steps:
1) I’ll schedule vendor intros: Hightouch + Census (30 min each). Target next week.
2) @David Kim can you share our current HubSpot API rate limits + what endpoints we’re hitting most? (so we can ask pointed questions)
3) @Tyler Benson + @Isabelle Laurent can you list the exact HubSpot properties/workflows that are “hands off” / sensitive (ownership, lifecycle stage, etc.)
4) @Dr. Aisha Rahman can we get a quick checklist for vendor security review (DPA, SOC2, retention, sub-processors)?

David Kim: yep. I’ll drop notes here later today + a link to the internal rate-limit incident ticket.

Tyler Benson: I’ll put together a “do not touch” list + the properties we’d *want* a vendor to populate (industry, employee band, HQ region, technographics maybe).

Isabelle Laurent: I’ll add the routing/ownership gotchas + which workflows can accidentally reassign.

Ben Carter: also, can we define what “success” is for a vendor?
- reduce eng time spent on enrichment by X hrs/week
- keep signal task creation success rate >99%
- no increase in complaint rate (unwanted outreach) due to bad enrichment

Camila Reyes: :thumbsup: will add to the eval doc.

Camila Reyes: scheduling: proposing Tue 11am PT for Hightouch, Thu 10am PT for Census. Anyone want to join live? (partnerships + ops + security would be good)

Dr. Aisha Rahman: I can do Thu.

David Kim: Tue works.

Tyler Benson: both work.

Ben Carter: I’ll join Tue.

Camila Reyes: cool I’ll send cal invites + add agenda/questions in the invite.

David Kim: (reaction) :white_check_mark:

Priya Natarajan: if the vendor route looks messy, we should be ready with a “build it ourselves” fallback: batcher + queue + suppression rules + minimal enrichment via our own lookup (domain -> region).

Camila Reyes: agreed. We’ll treat vendor as phase 2, not a blocker for signal routing MVP.

title: #partnerships: 1746204401
preview:
Ben Carter: dropping this here so we don’t keep re-litigating in DMs — can someone outline the AWS Marketplace *private offer* steps + what templates we need + who owns each step? Need this for the regulated ABM targets (finserv first) and co-sell motion.

Tyler Benson: yep. here’s the “happy path” checklist we’ve used before. will paste rough steps + owners, then we can turn into a one-pager.

Tyler Benson: **AWS MP Private Offer (CPPO) – steps (internal)**
1) Confirm we’re selling via AWS MP for this account (vs direct order form). Owner: AE + Partnerships.
2) Identify SKU / product listing in MP (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Private). Owner: Partnerships (w/ Product).
3) Create/associate the customer opp in ACE + tag for co-sell (if we want AWS overlay). Owner: Partnerships + AE.
4) Collect inputs for offer: legal entity, billing account ID, start date, term, region constraints, pricing (committed spend / monthly), any credits. Owner: AE + Deal Desk.
5) Draft private offer in Marketplace console (CPPO) w/ custom price + EULA references. Owner: Partnerships (builder).
6) Legal review: EULA / amendments + DPA linkage + any required security addendum. Owner: Legal.
7) Approvals: Finance (discount/credits), Security (if we’re attaching evidence pack / specific claims). Owner: AE drives.
8) Send offer to buyer in AWS MP, buyer accepts in their AWS account. Owner: AE shepherds.
9) Post-acceptance: confirm entitlement/fulfillment process (SaaS contract vs AMI/container delivery) + kickoff. Owner: CS/SE for onboarding.

Tyler Benson: **Templates / docs we typically need**
- “Private Offer Intake” (fields: customer AWS account ID, buyer email, PO#, billing entity, term, start date, residency requirements, SKU).
- Pricing approval snippet (credits/discount guardrails).
- EULA link + any standard addenda (DPA, SLA schedule references).
- Fulfillment checklist (who provisioned what + timelines).

Tyler Benson: i don’t have these in a single folder yet — if folks agree i’ll create a Drive folder and drop the intake template + checklist.

Sofia Mendes: adding guardrails: if we do CPPO with a regulated buyer, we still need the same approvals as direct, and we should avoid “free credits” language inside the offer itself. structure it as price reduction / committed spend where possible.

Sofia Mendes: also: for Dedicated vs Private, make sure the SKU language is consistent. i’ve seen people write “Private (on-prem)” in an AWS offer which is confusing since AWS expects a cloud fulfillment path. if it’s VPC on AWS that’s fine; on-prem should stay direct unless Legal signs off on delivery wording.

Ben Carter: super helpful. who *actually* clicks buttons in AWS MP console? partnerships or AEs?

Tyler Benson: partnerships should. AEs can’t always access the seller portal + it gets messy. AE owns the customer timeline + acceptance follow-up.

Michael Grant: legal notes (pls don’t skip):
- Marketplace private offers generally reference our standard EULA, but if the customer insists on papering redlines, we need to confirm whether AWS flow supports an amendment attachment vs separate doc.
- Data residency + retention language needs to match what we’ve already approved for regulated ABM. Don’t create bespoke promises in the offer text.
- If we attach a DPA, use the latest approved version + make sure we’re not contradicting AWS’s ordering doc terms.

Stephanie Nguyen: +1 on ownership: AE drives the deal; partnerships builds CPPO; Legal approves terms; Finance approves any discounts. for finserv targets we’ll almost always need security looped early bc procurement will ask.

Naomi Feldman: security plug: if the account is requesting “SOC2 report before offer” / “evidence pack before acceptance,” route through the evidence pack index + restricted sharing. don’t upload evidence into AWS MP attachments.

Ben Carter: ok. can someone list the exact inputs we need from the buyer for CPPO so SDR/AE can ask cleanly?

Tyler Benson: minimum we’ve needed:
- Buyer’s AWS account ID (12-digit) + payer account if different
- Buyer email tied to that AWS org
- Legal entity name + billing address
- Desired start date + term length
- Product/SKU + region(s)
- Any constraints: data residency, support tier, uptime/SLA schedule

Tyler Benson: if it’s a co-sell, also: ACE opp ID + AWS partner manager name.

Sofia Mendes: pricing inputs:
- monthly commit (or annual total) + ramp
- usage assumptions if we’re mapping to token-based spend (so we don’t underprice)
- whether this includes support/compliance add-ons

Ben Carter: can we get a canonical “CPPO intake form” doc? today preferably.

Tyler Benson: i’ll spin one up and drop link here EOD.

Michael Grant: please include a checkbox on the intake for “customer requested custom terms” + “customer requires DPA/SLA schedules” so we can triage quickly.

--- (6 days later) ---

Ben Carter: circling back — we did the AWS MP process call. adding transcript link here + key takeaways. https://app.fireflies.ai/view/2025-05-09-aws-marketplace-private-offer-process::redwood

Ben Carter: takeaway from AWS: they want us to pre-populate the offer w/ clean SKU naming + not overload the offer text w/ security claims; keep security in separate collateral. also: if we want co-sell support, ACE opp needs to be created *before* requesting AWS help.

Tyler Benson: intake template is live. (internal) https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1RedwoodAWSMP-PrivateOffer-Templates
- CPPO Intake (Google Doc)
- CPPO Checklist (1-pager)
- UTM + attribution notes for MP sourced pipeline

Sofia Mendes: reviewed intake. added a section for “Marketplace fees accounted for?” + “billing cadence (monthly vs annual upfront).”

Michael Grant: reviewed. added standard language note: “Do not represent HIPAA compliance / ‘HIPAA-ready’ in offer text.”

Stephanie Nguyen: this is solid. can we pin this thread + Tyler’s folder link in #sales too? we’re going to need it once Trident/Regal start asking for MP routes.

Tyler Benson: yep will cross-post and pin here. :thumbsup:

title: #partnerships: 1746557733
preview:
Ben Carter: pulling this into #partnerships — we should tag a subset of finserv ABM targets for AWS co-sell. Which accounts make sense + what proof points can we lean on for regulated workloads?

Ben Carter: link to target list (finserv + health tabs): https://drive.google.com/…/target-account-list-finserv-health-sheet

Tyler Benson: 👍 for co-sell tagging. From ops side: if we decide the list, I can add a “Partner motion = AWS co-sell” flag + stage in HubSpot. Also need a short “why AWS” note per account for the ACE submission.

Stephanie Nguyen: for NA, I’d start with the ones already asking VPC/private + procurement timeline <90 days.
- Northbridge Banking (active discovery + pilot motion)
- Orion Payments (already asked about VPC/private + regional isolation)
- Brightline Insurance (heavy security review, evidence pack requests)
- Regal Wealth Mgmt (deep dive on audit logs + KMS)
Trident Financial is later stage but might be better handled direct for now unless we want Marketplace as an easier paper path.

Avery Johnson: +1 on Northbridge + Brightline. Both mentioned “AWS internal standards” + their security teams asked about KMS ownership and audit export. That’s usually a good co-sell tell.

Lukas Meyer: EMEA note: if any of these have EU residency constraints, AWS co-sell can help if we’re anchoring on specific regions + Marketplace procurement. But I don’t want to overpromise “EU-only” if we don’t have it locked for their exact needs.

Hannah Schmitt: Lakeview Credit Union is still super early / no clear champion. I’d leave it off the co-sell list until we have confirmed AWS footprint + a named security owner.

Soojin Lee: proof points we can use (messaging-safe):
- “Supports Private deployment patterns (VPC / on-prem options) + customer-managed networking”
- “KMS/HSM integrations for key management” (don’t imply specific HSM vendors unless in the evidence pack)
- “Audit logs + export options” (again: keep to what’s documented)
- “SSO/SAML + RBAC”
- “Data retention controls & residency discussed case-by-case”
We should avoid: “HIPAA-ready” / “guaranteed compliance” language. For finserv, we can say “designed for regulated workloads” + “supports common controls.”

Ben Carter: helpful. for AWS co-sell specifically: do we have partner-ready assets beyond “we can transact via Marketplace private offer”?

Tyler Benson: we can point to the Marketplace process doc we captured from the partner call last week (private offer workflow). Also we’ve got the regulated landing page draft + security FAQ + evidence pack index. Not “partner-branded” but usable.

Stephanie Nguyen: if AWS wants a customer story, we don’t have a named finserv logo we can use externally. We can still provide generic proof points:
- benchmark + architecture validation as a short pilot
- Dedicated vs Private mapping (data isolation + predictable throughput)
- security posture (SOC2 process, encryption, audit logs)
But we should keep it “reference architectures + controls” vs “customer X did Y.”

Avery Johnson: on Orion: they specifically asked whether they can keep traffic in-region + what happens during capacity events. Redwood routing/fallback story is strong, but for regulated we need to be crisp about “fallback only within approved policy” (don’t imply cross-region failover unless configured).

Soojin Lee: +1. For partner proof points, focus on: procurement acceleration via Marketplace + “meets security teams where they are” (questionnaires, evidence pack, audit artifacts). Also: “Private deployments available for strict data boundary requirements.”

Hannah Schmitt: propose tagging list v1:
1) Northbridge Banking
2) Brightline Insurance
3) Orion Payments
4) Regal Wealth Mgmt
Optional / later: Trident Financial (if they ask for Marketplace path)
Not tagging: Lakeview CU (too early)

Tyler Benson: works. If you confirm v1 list, I’ll:
- update HubSpot company records with “Partner = AWS co-sell”
- add task template: “collect AWS footprint + procurement preference (Marketplace vs direct)”
- add UTM + source rules so we don’t mess up attribution :grimacing:

Ben Carter: confirmed. v1 list = those 4.
Action items:
- Tyler: tag + add fields/tasks today
- Ben: draft 1-paragraph “co-sell rationale” per account (AWS footprint + procurement angle)
- Soojin: drop a short “approved partner-safe proof points” blurb we can paste into the ACE notes (no risky claims)

Stephanie Nguyen: can we also include a note on who owns the AWS relationship? so sellers know where to route questions.

Ben Carter: good call. For now, route to me for coordination + I’ll loop in whoever needs to submit the ACE items. Also linking the Marketplace steps thread here: https://slack.com/…/1746204401

Tyler Benson: 👍 will post back once HubSpot updates are in.

Soojin Lee: I’ll send a paste-ready blurb in ~30 min. Keeping it to: deployment flexibility (VPC/on-prem), audit logging, customer-managed keys, SSO/SAML, retention controls (configurable), and “security evidence pack available under NDA.”

Hannah Schmitt: :thumbsup: thanks all

title: #partnerships: 1735012345
preview:
Ava Chen: Heads-up: Kestrel customer is targeting a Saturday go-live (UTC) and the SI needs an architecture sign-off before we handoff. Two immediate blockers: marketplace image rejected by signing policy, and customer legal wants explicit telemetry opt-in flow documented.

Ravi Patel: On marketplace image — can you paste the rejection log? I want to check if it's signing metadata or the provenance bundle.

Jules Park: Paste from the marketplace webhook: `status: rejected, reason: signature-mismatch, code: IMG_SIG_403`. Customer image was built by the SI with our base runtime + custom driver.

Jules Park: Repro steps we ran locally: `rsi-cli sign --key=kestrel-prod --image=si/custom-driver:v3 && rsi-cli verify --image=si/custom-driver:v3` -> verify fails with the same error.

Marcus Li: Possible cause: SI's CI re-bundled layers after signing (timestamped layers). Need to confirm their build pipeline order. Celeste, can your field eng contact the SI CI owner and confirm the sha flow?

Celeste (SI): On it. FYI our internal build uses `docker build --squash` which we suspect rewrites layer metadata. We can rework to sign after final push. Working with CI now.

Ava Chen: Good. Second blocker — telemetry consent. The customer's legal requires explicit opt-in UX + a retained list of data types sent to Redwood for diagnostics. They want a short doc + what fields we collect, retention window, and where in the app the user journey surfaces the opt-in.

Ravi Patel: For the doc, can we reuse the standard marketplace telemetry appendix and add two items: 1) `session_id` (hashed), 2) `payload_size` + `latency_ms`. Retention 30 days by default, 90 days on request.

Marcus Li: Confirmed we collect `session_id` (hashed), anonymized `model_variant`, token counts, and latency buckets. No raw PII unless customer opts in. I'll draft the appendix.

Celeste (SI): Customer also wants to know what happens if telemetry is disabled — does the SI still get error telemetry? They want guaranteed minimal logs for support triage.

Ava Chen: We need an explicit policy: if telemetry disabled, we fall back to anonymized failure counters and ephemeral traces (no payloads). Add that to the doc and to the canary playbook.

Jules Park: About the signing flow — temporary workaround: we can publish a compatibility image in our marketplace that wraps the SI image and includes our verified signature layer. But that requires a quick image rewrite and another verification cycle.

Ravi Patel: Time estimate for wrap-and-verify? If we automate with `rsi-wrapper` it should be ~2 hours (build + verify + submit). But we should avoid last-minute churn during the Saturday slot.

Ava Chen: Plan proposal: 1) Celeste to confirm SI CI fix by EOD (GMT), 2) Marcus to draft telemetry appendix by EOD, 3) Jules to try wrapper fallback in a staging run and report by 10:00 UTC tomorrow, 4) If SI fix not ready, use wrapper and schedule a patch window Sunday morning.

Celeste (SI): CI owner responded — will move signing step after final push. ETA: 6 hours. I pushed a note to the SI channel and CC'd their CI lead.

deploy-bot: [staging-checker] marketplace-verify job started for si/custom-driver:v3 (run: 2024-08-15T22:04Z)

Jules Park: Staging run started — attaching short log snippet: `VERIFY: signature ok on layer 1, mismatch on manifest.json` looks like manifest canonicalization difference. Trying with `--canonicalize=true`.

Marcus Li: Draft telemetry appendix is here: https://redwood-internal/docs/marketplace/telemetry-appendix-draft (private). Will share a lightweight markdown to paste into the partner contract.

Ava Chen: Quick decisions: do we require SI to make the manifest change or do we accept wrapper for this go-live? I prefer SI fix for long-term, wrapper for short-term only. Anyone oppose? :thumbsup:

Ravi Patel: +1 for that.

Celeste (SI): SI agrees to fix manifest. They asked if Redwood can provide a one-line doc snippet for canonical manifest formatting.

Jules Park: I posted a minimal `manifest.json` example in the channel: `{ "schemaVersion": 2, "config": { ... }, "layers": [...] }` and the `--canonicalize` flag we used. I'll paste as snippet to SI.

Ava Chen: OK. Final: I'll update the go-live runbook to include the wrapper contingency, add the telemetry appendix link, and mark SI CI owner as responsible. If CI fix confirmed in 6 hours, we proceed with Saturday. If not, wrapper + Sunday patch.

Celeste (SI): Noted. I'll update the SI and confirm once their CI pipeline has the signing step moved.

Ravi Patel: I'll queue the small policy note with legal for telemetry appendix signoff.

Ava Chen: Thanks all — please react with :eyes: when you've completed your action items.

Marcus Li: :eyes: (telemetry draft uploaded)

Jules Park: :eyes: (staging wrapper test done, results in the channel)

Celeste (SI): :eyes: (CI change committed)

Ava Chen: Closing thread — will reopen if any new blocker emerges.
text:
Ava Chen: Heads-up: Kestrel customer is targeting a Saturday go-live (UTC) and the SI needs an architecture sign-off before we handoff. Two immediate blockers: marketplace image rejected by signing policy, and customer legal wants explicit telemetry opt-in flow documented.

Ravi Patel: On marketplace image — can you paste the rejection log? I want to check if it's signing metadata or the provenance bundle.

Jules Park: Paste from the marketplace webhook: `status: rejected, reason: signature-mismatch, code: IMG_SIG_403`. Customer image was built by the SI with our base runtime + custom driver.

Jules Park: Repro steps we ran locally: `rsi-cli sign --key=kestrel-prod --image=si/custom-driver:v3 && rsi-cli verify --image=si/custom-driver:v3` -> verify fails with the same error.

Marcus Li: Possible cause: SI's CI re-bundled layers after signing (timestamped layers). Need to confirm their build pipeline order. Celeste, can your field eng contact the SI CI owner and confirm the sha flow?

Celeste (SI): On it. FYI our internal build uses `docker build --squash` which we suspect rewrites layer metadata. We can rework to sign after final push. Working with CI now.

Ava Chen: Good. Second blocker — telemetry consent. The customer's legal requires explicit opt-in UX + a retained list of data types sent to Redwood for diagnostics. They want a short doc + what fields we collect, retention window, and where in the app the user journey surfaces the opt-in.

Ravi Patel: For the doc, can we reuse the standard marketplace telemetry appendix and add two items: 1) `session_id` (hashed), 2) `payload_size` + `latency_ms`. Retention 30 days by default, 90 days on request.

Marcus Li: Confirmed we collect `session_id` (hashed), anonymized `model_variant`, token counts, and latency buckets. No raw PII unless customer opts in. I'll draft the appendix.

Celeste (SI): Customer also wants to know what happens if telemetry is disabled — does the SI still get error telemetry? They want guaranteed minimal logs for support triage.

Ava Chen: We need an explicit policy: if telemetry disabled, we fall back to anonymized failure counters and ephemeral traces (no payloads). Add that to the doc and to the canary playbook.

Jules Park: About the signing flow — temporary workaround: we can publish a compatibility image in our marketplace that wraps the SI image and includes our verified signature layer. But that requires a quick image rewrite and another verification cycle.

Ravi Patel: Time estimate for wrap-and-verify? If we automate with `rsi-wrapper` it should be ~2 hours (build + verify + submit). But we should avoid last-minute churn during the Saturday slot.

Ava Chen: Plan proposal: 1) Celeste to confirm SI CI fix by EOD (GMT), 2) Marcus to draft telemetry appendix by EOD, 3) Jules to try wrapper fallback in a staging run and report by 10:00 UTC tomorrow, 4) If SI fix not ready, use wrapper and schedule a patch window Sunday morning.

Celeste (SI): CI owner responded — will move signing step after final push. ETA: 6 hours. I pushed a note to the SI channel and CC'd their CI lead.

deploy-bot: [staging-checker] marketplace-verify job started for si/custom-driver:v3 (run: 2024-08-15T22:04Z)

Jules Park: Staging run started — attaching short log snippet: `VERIFY: signature ok on layer 1, mismatch on manifest.json` looks like manifest canonicalization difference. Trying with `--canonicalize=true`.

Marcus Li: Draft telemetry appendix is here: https://redwood-internal/docs/marketplace/telemetry-appendix-draft (private). Will share a lightweight markdown to paste into the partner contract.

Ava Chen: Quick decisions: do we require SI to make the manifest change or do we accept wrapper for this go-live? I prefer SI fix for long-term, wrapper for short-term only. Anyone oppose? :thumbsup:

Ravi Patel: +1 for that.

Celeste (SI): SI agrees to fix manifest. They asked if Redwood can provide a one-line doc snippet for canonical manifest formatting.

Jules Park: I posted a minimal `manifest.json` example in the channel: `{ "schemaVersion": 2, "config": { ... }, "layers": [...] }` and the `--canonicalize` flag we used. I'll paste as snippet to SI.

Ava Chen: OK. Final: I'll update the go-live runbook to include the wrapper contingency, add the telemetry appendix link, and mark SI CI owner as responsible. If CI fix confirmed in 6 hours, we proceed with Saturday. If not, wrapper + Sunday patch.

Celeste (SI): Noted. I'll update the SI and confirm once their CI pipeline has the signing step moved.

Ravi Patel: I'll queue the small policy note with legal for telemetry appendix signoff.

Ava Chen: Thanks all — please react with :eyes: when you've completed your action items.

Marcus Li: :eyes: (telemetry draft uploaded)

Jules Park: :eyes: (staging wrapper test done, results in the channel)

Celeste (SI): :eyes: (CI change committed)

Ava Chen: Closing thread — will reopen if any new blocker emerges.

title: #partnerships: 1718933333
preview:
Maya Lopez: Hey team, kick-off for a small joint perf pilot with CascadeCompute. Goal: optimize perf/W and validate throughput stability at 2.5k toks/s on 12k context. Also want a short partner-ready blurb if results look good.
Ben Carter: Sounds good. Measurement plan idea: p50/p95 latency, sustained throughput over 30m windows, rack-level and per-GPU power. Also capture thermal throttling events.
Anika - CascadeCompute: We can provide per-board ammeter readings and chassis thermals. Our baseline is ~2.0k toks/s @ 280W (PDU measured). We'll throw our kernel engs at it.
Maya Lopez: Nice. Keep public claims conservative. Marketing (Jules) please note: prefer relative uplift phrasing, no absolute SLA commitments.
Jules (Marketing): Got it. We want one hero perf/W vs power curve and a short callout comparing "stock" vs "tuned" configs. I'll prepare a template blurb once we have preliminary numbers.
Ben Carter: Technical plan: 1) baseline run (stock kernel, no quant changes), 2) microkernel tweaks (inline fused-attn + memory packing), 3) scheduler tweak (batching policy), 4) long stability run (2 hours). Capture CSV with timestamped samples.
Anika - CascadeCompute: We'll mirror the same model binary and bench harness. Question: do you want raw CSVs or pre-aggregated metrics?
Ben Carter: Raw timestamped CSVs preferred + optional parquet for fast analysis. Include sample frequency (e.g., 1s) and UTC timestamps.
Maya Lopez: Ops-bot, can you reserve a bench window tomorrow 08:30 PT? Also tag eng-infra.
ops-bot: bench-scheduler: Reserved bench slot: 2024-05-14 08:30 PT. Notifying #eng-infra.
Anika - CascadeCompute: We'll aim to upload PDU logs and per-board traces before the run. Also will share kernel patches internally for review.
Ben Carter: I'll prepare a minimal config snippet to run on your side. Sharing now:
```bash
# quick bench config
export RW_BENCH_SEQ=12288
./runbench --model redwood-optim --bs 1 --quant q4_0 --kernel-flag fused_attn=1 --out csv
```
Jules (Marketing): Timeline proposal: dry-run tomorrow, tuned runs next Tue/Wed, internal review Thu, draft marketplace copy by Fri. Target soft partner announcement in ~2 weeks if green.
Maya Lopez: Action items: Ben to post run script and analysis notebook; Anika to upload baseline telemetry; Jules to draft guarded blurb; Maya to circulate partner publicity checklist and legal contact. Post summaries back here.
Ben Carter: Will upload baseline CSV by EOD today and PR for kernel tweak branch.
Anika - CascadeCompute: I'll send network access details for the shared bucket and confirm engs.
Jules (Marketing): I'll also prepare a short "customer benefits" line we can approve quickly if numbers look clean.
Maya Lopez: Thanks all. If we hit >=12% perf/W uplift with no quality regressions, we move to a joint case study. Keep the thread tidy and avoid external sharing until partner sign-off. :handshake:

title: #partnerships: 1718700000
preview:
Maya Chen: Heads-up — we need to lock the QBR pack for our cloud marketplace + hardware partners by EOD Thurs. Main asks: updated scorecards, proposed tier changes for three partners, and MDF allocation for H2.

Sanjay Patel: Got it. Can you share the scorecard template you want us to use?

Maya Chen: Use the one from last quarter but add a row for 'Marketplace onboarding delta' and 'joint-announcement readiness'. Quick snippet: 
```
Metrics: revenue, marketplace listings, joint deals, enablement hours, marketplace onboarding delta, NPS change
Tier thresholds: Platinum >= $1.5M ARR or 20 joint deals; Gold >= $500k ARR or 5 joint deals
```

Olivia Ramos: I pulled partner performance for Q1-Q2. Will attach the CSV in the doc. A couple of notes: VendorX is borderline Gold->Platinum based on pipeline; HardwareCo needs MDF justification — underutilized last quarter.

Ben Carter: For MDF, propose splitting $150k available: $80k for HardwareCo (co-marketing + PoC support), $50k for VendorX (marketplace listing campaigns), $20k for channel enablement credits. Thoughts? :thinking_face:

Kate Liu: @Ben that sounds reasonable. Can we tie the HardwareCo $80k to specific milestones? e.g., 2 joint webinars, 1 PoC completed, 3 case study leads. Payments on milestone completion.

Sanjay Patel: Agree on milestones. Also propose a deferred tranche for underperforming partners — hold 25% until they hit onboarding metrics. Helps reduce waste.

Maya Chen: Good call. For the QBR slides I'll include a slide that maps MDF -> KPIs and payment schedule. Olivia, can you flag any partners that should be demoted in tiers? We need to be candid for exec review.

Olivia Ramos: HardwareCo has 3 months of stalled integrations and only 1 marketplace listing. Recommend keeping Gold but downgrade their co-sell priority. VendorY should be demoted (low engagement, low deal velocity).

Ben Carter: I'll draft the budget allocation table and include the milestone payment rows. Will post a Google Sheet link in 30 mins. :link:

marketing-bot: FYI — press checklist for joint announcements: announcement copy, executive quotes, customer references, approved creatives, regional cloud marketplace banners. Deadline for final assets: 5 business days before publish.

Maya Chen: Perfect. Let's run through owners: Scorecards — Olivia. Tiers & demotions — Maya + Olivia. MDF budget + milestones — Ben. QBR slide deck assembly — me + Kate. Communication plan for announcements — Olivia + Marketing.

Sanjay Patel: Can we set a short pre-QBR sync tomorrow 10am to review finalized scorecards and MDF table?

Maya Chen: Yes. Calendar invite sent. Please bring any objections to tier changes then. Thanks everyone. :thumbsup:
